Kikuyu: Inyuĩ hinga ici! Nĩmũũĩ wega gũkũũrana ũrĩa igũrũ na thĩ gũtariĩ. Mũkĩremagwo nĩgũkũũrana ũhoro wa mahinda maya tũrĩ nĩkĩ?
English: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky, but how is it that you don’t interpret this time?

Kikuyu: “Mwagaga gwĩtuĩra inyuĩ ene ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire nĩkĩ?
English: “Why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa mũrathiĩ na thũ yaku kũrĩ mũciirithania-rĩ, geria mũno wĩiguithanie nayo mũrĩ o njĩra-inĩ, ndĩgagũkururie ĩgũtware kũrĩ mũciirithania, nake mũciirithania akũneane kũrĩ mũnene, nake mũnene ũcio agũikie njeera.
English: For when you are going with your adversary before the magistrate, try diligently on the way to be released from him, lest perhaps he drag you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.

Kikuyu: Ngũkwĩra atĩrĩ, ndũkoima kuo ũtarĩhĩte thiirĩ wothe, hatarĩ gathendi ũgũtigia.”
English: I tell you, you will by no means get out of there until you have paid the very last penny.†”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩ kwarĩ na andũ amwe maarĩ ho hĩndĩ ĩyo, arĩa meerire Jesũ ũhoro wa andũ a Galili arĩa mooragĩtwo nĩ Pilato makĩruta magongona, nayo thakame yao igĩtukana na ya nyamũ cia magongona mao.
English: Now there were some present at the same time who told him about the Galileans whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Anga mũgwĩciiria atĩ andũ acio maanyariirirwo ũguo tondũ acio maarĩ ehia gũkĩra andũ acio angĩ othe a Galili?
English: Jesus answered them, “Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered such things?

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, aca! No rĩrĩ, mwaga kwĩrira, o na inyuĩ inyuothe mũkaniinwo o tao.
English: I tell you, no, but unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.

Kikuyu: Kana andũ arĩa ikũmi na anana, arĩa magwĩrĩirwo nĩ nyũmba ĩrĩa ndaaya kũu itũũra rĩa Siloamu ĩkĩmooraga-rĩ, anga mũgwĩciiria atĩ maarĩ na mahĩtia maingĩ gũkĩra andũ arĩa angĩ othe maatũũraga kũu Jerusalemu?
English: Or those eighteen on whom the tower in Siloam fell and killed them—do you think that they were worse offenders than all the men who dwell in Jerusalem?

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, aca! No rĩrĩ, mwaga kwĩrira, o na inyuĩ inyuothe mũkaaniinwo o tao.”
English: I tell you, no, but, unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmaarĩria na ngerekano, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũmwe aarĩ na mũkũyũ wahandĩtwo thĩinĩ wa mũgũnda wake wa mĩthabibũ, nake agĩthiĩ gũcaria maciaro mũtĩ-inĩ ũcio, no ndaigana kuona ngũyũ o na ĩmwe.
English: He spoke this parable. “A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit on it and found none.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, akĩĩra mũruti wa wĩra wa mũgũnda ũcio wa mĩthabibũ atĩrĩ, ‘Rĩu nĩ mĩaka ĩtatũ njũkaga gũcaria maciaro mũkũyũ-inĩ, ũyũ na ndirĩ ndoona ngũyũ o na ĩmwe. Ũteme! Ũũthũkagĩria mũgũnda nĩkĩ?’
English: He said to the vine dresser, ‘Behold, these three years I have come looking for fruit on this fig tree, and found none. Cut it down! Why does it waste the soil?’

Kikuyu: “No mũndũ ũcio akĩmũcookeria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathi ũyũ, nĩtũreke ũikare mwaka ũngĩ ũmwe, ndĩũrĩmĩre na ndĩwĩkĩre thumu.
English: He answered, ‘Lord, leave it alone this year also, until I dig around it and fertilize it.

Kikuyu: Naguo ũngĩciara hĩndĩ ĩyo, nĩ wega! No ũngĩaga gũciara, no ũtemwo.’ ”
English: If it bears fruit, fine; but if not, after that, you can cut it down.’ ”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya ũmwe wa Thabatũ, Jesũ nĩkũrutana aarutanaga thĩinĩ wa thunagogi ĩmwe,
English: He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath day.

Kikuyu: nakuo gũgĩkorwo nĩ kwarĩ na mũtumia wonjetio nĩ ngoma thũku mĩaka ikũmi na ĩnana. Atũire ainamĩrĩire atekũhota gwĩtiira o na hanini.
English: Behold, there was a woman who had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years. She was bent over and could in no way straighten herself up.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Jesũ aamuonire, akĩmwĩta, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũtumia ũyũ nĩwohorwo kuuma kũrĩ wonje waku.”
English: When Jesus saw her, he called her and said to her, “Woman, you are freed from your infirmity.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmũigĩrĩra moko, na kahinda o kau agĩĩtiira, na akĩgooca Ngai.
English: He laid his hands on her, and immediately she stood up straight and glorified God.

Kikuyu: Nake mũnene wa thunagogi akĩrakara mũno nĩ ũndũ wa Jesũ kũhonania mũthenya wa Thabatũ. Nake akĩĩra andũ atĩrĩ, “He na mĩthenya ĩtandatũ ya kũruta wĩra. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ũkagai kũhonio mĩthenya ĩyo, no ti mũthenya wa Thabatũ.”
English: The ruler of the synagogue, being indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, said to the multitude, “There are six days in which men ought to work. Therefore come on those days and be healed, and not on the Sabbath day!”

Kikuyu: Nowe Mwathani akĩmũcookeria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ hinga ici! Githĩ o mũndũ wanyu ndohoraga ndegwa yake kana ndigiri yake yume kiugũ mũthenya wa Thabatũ, amĩtware ĩthiĩ ĩkanyue maaĩ?
English: Therefore the Lord answered him, “You hypocrites! Doesn’t each one of you free his ox or his donkey from the stall on the Sabbath and lead him away to water?

Kikuyu: Nake mũtumia ũyũ mwarĩ wa Iburahĩmu, ũrĩa ũtũire ohetwo nĩ Shaitani mĩaka ikũmi na ĩnana; ndakĩagĩrĩrwo nĩ kuohorwo o na kũrĩ mũthenya wa Thabatũ?”
English: Ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham whom Satan had bound eighteen long years, be freed from this bondage on the Sabbath day?”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa oigire ũguo, thũ ciake ciothe igĩconoka, no kĩrĩndĩ kĩu kĩa andũ gĩgĩkena nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ ma magegania marĩa eekaga.
English: As he said these things, all his adversaries were disappointed; and all the multitude rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jesũ akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Ũthamaki wa Ngai ũhaanaine na kĩ? Na ngũũgerekania na kĩĩ?
English: He said, “What is God’s Kingdom like? To what shall I compare it?

Kikuyu: Ũhaana ta mbeũ ya karatarĩ ĩrĩa mũndũ oire akĩhaanda mũgũnda wake. Igĩkũra ĩgĩtuĩka mũtĩ, nacio nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ igĩũka igĩaka itara honge-inĩ ciaguo.”
English: It is like a grain of mustard seed which a man took and put in his own garden. It grew and became a large tree, and the birds of the sky live in its branches.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Ingĩgerekania ũthamaki wa Ngai na kĩĩ?
English: Again he said, “To what shall I compare God’s Kingdom?

Kikuyu: Ũhaana ta ndawa ya kũimbia mĩgate ĩrĩa mũndũ-wa-nja ooire na akĩmĩtukania na mũtu mũingĩ, o nginya wothe ũkĩimba.”
English: It is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in three measures † of flour, until it was all leavened.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩthiĩ ahĩtũkĩire matũũra-inĩ na tũtũũra-inĩ akĩrutanaga erekeire Jerusalemu.
English: He went on his way through cities and villages, teaching, and traveling on to Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Nake mũndũ ũmwe akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani anga no andũ anini tu makahonokio?” Nakeakĩmeeraatĩrĩ,
English: One said to him, “Lord, are they few who are saved?” He said to them,

Kikuyu: “Geragiainahinyagũtoonyeramũrangoũrĩamũceke; tondũngũmwĩraatĩrĩ, andũ aingĩ nĩ makaageria gũtoonya, no matikahota.
English: “Strive to enter in by the narrow door, for many, I tell you, will seek to enter in and will not be able.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mwene nyũmba agaakorwo okĩrĩte na ahinge mũrango-rĩ, mũkaarũgama hau nja mũkĩringaringaga mũrango mũkĩmũthaithaga, mũkoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathi witũ, tũhingũrĩre mũrango.’ “Nake nĩakamũcookeria amwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Niĩ ndimũũĩ o na ndiũĩ kũrĩa muumĩte.’
English: When once the master of the house has risen up and has shut the door, and you begin to stand outside and to knock at the door, saying, ‘Lord, Lord, open to us!’ then he will answer and tell you, ‘I don’t know you or where you come from.’

Kikuyu: “Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũkoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩtwarĩĩanagĩra na tũkanyuuanĩra nawe, o na nĩwarutanaga njĩra-inĩ cia matũũra maitũ.’
English: Then you will begin to say, ‘We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.’

Kikuyu: “Nowe nĩakamacookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Niĩ ndimũũĩ, o na ndiũĩ kũrĩa muumĩte. Njehererai, inyuĩ inyuothe mwĩkaga ũũru!’
English: He will say, ‘I tell you, I don’t know where you come from. Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity.’

Kikuyu: “Kũu nĩgũkagĩa na kĩrĩro, na kũhagarania magego, rĩrĩa mũkoona Iburahĩmu, na Isaaka, na Jakubu, na anabii othe marĩ ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai, no inyuĩ mũkorwo mũikĩtio nja.
English: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God’s Kingdom, and yourselves being thrown outside.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ nĩmagooka kuuma mwena wa irathĩro, na wa ithũĩro, na wa gathigathini, na wa gũthini, maikare thĩ iruga-inĩ kũu ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai.
English: They will come from the east, west, north, and south, and will sit down in God’s Kingdom.

Kikuyu: No ti-itherũ, nĩ kũrĩ andũ marĩ thuutha magaatuĩka a mbere, na nĩ kũrĩ a mbere magaatuĩka a thuutha.”
English: Behold, there are some who are last who will be first, and there are some who are first who will be last.”

Kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo Afarisai amwe magĩũka kũrĩ Jesũ makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Uma gũkũ ũthiĩ kũndũ kũngĩ. Herode nĩarenda gũkũũragithia.”
English: On that same day, some Pharisees came, saying to him, “Get out of here and go away, for Herod wants to kill you.”

Kikuyu: Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi mũkeere mbwe ĩyo atĩrĩ, ‘Ũmũthĩ na rũciũ ngũkorwo ngĩingata ndaimono na ngĩhonia andũ, na mũthenya wa ĩtatũ ndĩĩkie wĩra wakwa.’
English: He said to them, “Go and tell that fox, ‘Behold, I cast out demons and perform cures today and tomorrow, and the third day I complete my mission.

Kikuyu: O na kũrĩ ũguo, ũmũthĩ na rũciũ o na oke no nginya ngorwo ngĩthiĩ o na mbere, nĩgũkorwo ti-itherũ gũtirĩ mũnabii ũngĩkuĩra nja ya Jerusalemu!
English: Nevertheless I must go on my way today and tomorrow and the next day, for it can’t be that a prophet would perish outside of Jerusalem.’

Kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, wee Jerusalemu, Jerusalemu, wee ũragaga anabii, na ũkahũũra na mahiga nyuguto arĩa matũmĩtwo kũrĩ we, nĩ maita maingĩ atĩa ndanenda gũcookanĩrĩria ciana ciaku hamwe o ta ũrĩa ngũkũ ĩcookanagĩrĩria tũcui twayo mathagu-inĩ mayo, no ndũnjĩtĩkagĩria!
English: “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her! How often I wanted to gather your children together, like a hen gathers her own brood under her wings, and you refused!

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, nyũmba yaku ĩiyũrĩte ihooru. Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, mũtikanyona rĩngĩ o nginya rĩrĩa mũkoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũgũũka na rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani.’ ”
English: Behold, your house is left to you desolate. I tell you, you will not see me until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!’ ” *

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya ũmwe wa Thabatũ, Jesũ nĩathiire kũrĩa irio nyũmba ya mũnene ũmwe wa Afarisai, nao andũ maikarĩte mamũrorete mũno.
English: When he went into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees on a Sabbath to eat bread, they were watching him.

Kikuyu: Na hau mbere yake nĩ haarĩ na mũndũ warũarĩte mũrimũ wa kũimba mwĩrĩ.
English: Behold, a certain man who had dropsy was in front of him.

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩũria Afarisai na arutani a watho atĩrĩ, “Watho nĩwĩtĩkĩrĩtie kũhonania mũthenya wa Thabatũ, kana ndwĩtĩkĩrĩtie?”
English: Jesus, answering, spoke to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”

Kikuyu: No-o magĩkira ki. Nake akĩnyiita mũndũ ũcio, akĩmũhonia na akĩmwĩra athiĩ.
English: But they were silent. He took him, and healed him, and let him go.

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ wanyu ũngĩaga kũruta mũriũ irima, kana ndegwa yake ĩgwĩte irima rĩa maaĩ mũthenya wa Thabatũ?”
English: He answered them, “Which of you, if your son† or an ox fell into a well, wouldn’t immediately pull him out on a Sabbath day?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩaga ũndũ wa kũmũcookeria.
English: They couldn’t answer him regarding these things.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ oonire ũrĩa ageni maathuuraga itĩ iria ciaikaragĩrwo nĩ ageni a gũtĩĩo maikarĩre cio- rĩ, akĩmahe ngerekano ĩno, akiuga atĩrĩ:
English: He spoke a parable to those who were invited, when he noticed how they chose the best seats, and said to them,

Kikuyu: “Mũndũ angĩgwĩta iruga-inĩ rĩa kĩhikanio-rĩ, ndũgaikarĩre itĩ iria ciigĩirwo ageni a gũtĩĩo, tondũ no hakorwo mũndũ ũngĩ mũtĩĩe gũgũkĩra wĩtĩtwo nĩ mwene iruga.
English: “When you are invited by anyone to a wedding feast, don’t sit in the best seat, since perhaps someone more honorable than you might be invited by him,

Kikuyu: Kũngĩkĩhaana ũguo-rĩ, mwene iruga ũrĩa ũmwĩtĩte inyuĩ eerĩ no ooke, akwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Eherera mũndũ ũyũ gĩtĩ kĩu.’ Hĩndĩ ĩyo, nĩũgaaikara thuutha biũ ũconokete.
English: and he who invited both of you would come and tell you, ‘Make room for this person.’ Then you would begin, with shame, to take the lowest place.

Kikuyu: No hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ũngĩtwo-rĩ, ikaragĩra itĩ cia thuutha, nĩgeetha ũrĩa ũgwĩtĩte ooka, akwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrata wakwa, thiĩ ũgaikare harĩa mbere.’ Hĩndĩĩyonĩũkaheogĩtĩĩo maitho-inĩma ageniarĩa angĩothe.
English: But when you are invited, go and sit in the lowest place, so that when he who invited you comes, he may tell you, ‘Friend, move up higher.’ Then you will be honored in the presence of all who sit at the table with you.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwomũndũũrĩawothe wĩtũgagĩria nĩakanyiihio, na ũrĩa wothe wĩnyiihagia nĩagatũũgĩrio.”
English: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcooka akĩĩra mũndũ ũcio wamwĩtĩte atĩrĩ, “Waruga iruga rĩa mũthenya kana rĩa hwaĩ-inĩ- rĩ, ndũgetage o arata aku kana ariũ a thoguo, kana andũ anyu, o na kana andũ arĩa atongu a itũũra rĩanyu; ũngĩka ũguo-rĩ, nao nĩmagagwĩta na nĩ ũndũ ũcio makũrĩhe ũguo ũmekĩte.
English: He also said to the one who had invited him, “When you make a dinner or a supper, don’t call your friends, nor your brothers, nor your kinsmen, nor rich neighbors, or perhaps they might also return the favor, and pay you back.

Kikuyu: No hĩndĩ ĩrĩa waruga iruga ĩtaga athĩĩni, na cionje, na ithua, na atumumu,
English: But when you make a feast, ask the poor, the maimed, the lame, or the blind;

Kikuyu: nawe nĩũkarathimwo. O na gũtuĩka matingĩhota gũkũrĩha, nĩũkarĩhwo hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ arĩa athingu makaariũkio.”
English: and you will be blessed, because they don’t have the resources to repay you. For you will be repaid in the resurrection of the righteous.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa mũndũ ũmwe wa arĩa maikarĩte metha-inĩ hamwe nake aiguire ũguo, akĩĩra Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũkaarĩa irio iruga-inĩ rĩa ũthamaki wa Ngai.”
English: When one of those who sat at the table with him heard these things, he said to him, “Blessed is he who will feast in God’s Kingdom!”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũmwe nĩahaarĩirie iruga inene na agĩĩta ageni aingĩ.
English: But he said to him, “A certain man made a great supper, and he invited many people.

Kikuyu: Nacio thaa cia kũrĩa ciakinya, agĩtũma ndungata yake ĩkeere andũ arĩa meetĩtwo atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkai, nĩgũkorwo maũndũ mothe nĩmahaarĩrie.’
English: He sent out his servant at supper time to tell those who were invited, ‘Come, for everything is ready now.’

Kikuyu: “Nao othe, o mũndũ akĩambĩrĩria kũheana ciĩgwatio. Wa mbere akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘No hĩndĩ ndĩragũrire mũgũnda na no nginya thiĩ ngawone. Thiĩ ũmwĩre andekere.’
English: They all as one began to make excuses. “The first said to him, ‘I have bought a field, and I must go and see it. Please have me excused.’

Kikuyu: “Ũrĩa ũngĩ akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘No hĩndĩ ndĩragũrire ndegwa ikũmi cia kũrĩma, na nĩndĩrathiĩ gũcigeria. Thiĩ ũmwĩre andekere.’
English: “Another said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I must go try them out. Please have me excused.’

Kikuyu: “Nake ũngĩ akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘No hĩndĩ ndĩrahikanirie, nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndiihota gũũka.’
English: “Another said, ‘I have married a wife, and therefore I can’t come.’

Kikuyu: “Ndungata ĩyo ĩgĩthiĩ, ĩkĩmenyithia mwathi wayo ũhoro ũcio. Hĩndĩ ĩyo mwene mũciĩ akĩrakara na agĩatha ndungata ĩyo akĩmĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Umagara narua ũthiĩ njĩra-inĩ iria nene, o na tũcĩra-inĩ twa itũũra, ũrehe athĩĩni, na cionje, na arĩa atumumu, o na ithua.’
English: “That servant came, and told his lord these things. Then the master of the house, being angry, said to his servant, ‘Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in the poor, maimed, blind, and lame.’

Kikuyu: “Nayo ndungata ĩyo ĩkiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathi wakwa, ũrĩa wathanire nĩwĩkĩtwo, na no hatigaire handũ.’
English: “The servant said, ‘Lord, it is done as you commanded, and there is still room.’

Kikuyu: “Hĩndĩ ĩyo Mwathi wayo akĩĩra ndungata ĩyo atĩrĩ, ‘Umagara ũthiĩ rĩu njĩra-inĩ na ndeere-inĩ cia mĩgũnda, ũkaringĩrĩrie andũ moke nĩgeetha nyũmba yakwa ĩiyũre.
English: “The lord said to the servant, ‘Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wa acio metĩtwo ũgaacama iruga rĩakwa.’ ”
English: For I tell you that none of those men who were invited will taste of my supper. For many are called, but few are chosen.‡’ ”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, andũ aingĩ nĩmatwaranire na Jesũ, nake akĩmahũgũkĩra, akĩmeera atĩrĩ,
English: Now great multitudes were going with him. He turned and said to them,

Kikuyu: “Mũndũ o wothe angĩũka kũrĩ niĩ na aage gũthũũra ithe na nyina, na mũtumia wake, na ciana ciake, na ariũ a nyina, na aarĩ a nyina, o na muoyo wake we mwene-rĩ, ndangĩhota gũtuĩka mũrutwo wakwa.
English: “If anyone comes to me, and doesn’t disregard§ his own father, mother, wife, children, brothers, and sisters, yes, and his own life also, he can’t be my disciple.

Kikuyu: Nake mũndũ o wothe ũtegwĩkuuĩra mũtĩ wake wa kwambĩrwo anũmĩrĩre, ndangĩhota gũtuĩka mũrutwo wakwa.
English: Whoever doesn’t bear his own cross and come after me, can’t be my disciple.

Kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, mũndũ ũmwe wanyu angĩenda gwaka nyũmba ndaaya na igũrũ-rĩ, githĩ ndangĩamba gũikara thĩ atare thogora nĩguo one kana wĩra na mbeeca cia kũmĩrĩkia?
English: For which of you, desiring to build a tower, doesn’t first sit down and count the cost, to see if he has enough to complete it?

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo angĩremwo nĩ kũmĩrĩkia thuutha wa gwaka mũthingi, andũ arĩa othe marĩmĩonaga nĩmarĩmũthekagĩrĩra,
English: Or perhaps, when he has laid a foundation and isn’t able to finish, everyone who sees begins to mock him,

Kikuyu: makoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Mũndũ ũyũ aambĩrĩirie gwaka nyũmba akĩremwo nĩ kũmĩrĩkia.’
English: saying, ‘This man began to build and wasn’t able to finish.’

Kikuyu: “Kana mũthamaki angĩkorwo akiriĩ gũthiĩ kũrũa mbaara na mũthamaki ũngĩ-rĩ, githĩ ndan- gĩamba gũikara thĩ ecũũranie kana ahota kũrũa arĩ na andũ ngiri ikũmi na mũthamaki ũrĩa ũroka kũmũũkĩrĩra arĩ na andũ ngiri mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ?
English: Or what king, as he goes to encounter another king in war, will not sit down first and consider whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand?

Kikuyu: Angĩona atĩ ndangĩhota kũmũtooria, angĩtũmĩra mũthamaki ũcio ũngĩ andũ arĩ o kũraya mathiĩ makamũũrie kũgĩe na thayũ.
English: Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sends an envoy and asks for conditions of peace.

Kikuyu: Ũguo noguo, mũndũ o wothe wanyu angĩaga kũrekia indo ciothe iria arĩ nacio, ndangĩtuĩka mũrutwo wakwa.
English: So therefore, whoever of you who doesn’t renounce all that he has, he can’t be my disciple.

Kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, cumbĩ nĩ mwega, no cumbĩ ũngĩkorwo nĩũthirĩte mũcamo waguo-rĩ, ũngĩcooka gũcamithio ta cumbĩ na kĩ?
English: “Salt is good, but if the salt becomes flat and tasteless, with what do you season it?

Kikuyu: Cumbĩ ũcio ndũrĩ bata gũitwo tĩĩri-inĩ o na kana gũitwo kĩara-inĩ, nĩgũteeo ũteagwo. “Ũrĩa ũrĩ na matũ ma kũigua, nĩakĩigue.”
English: It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. It is thrown out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, etia mbeeca cia igooti na “ehia” othe nĩmoonganaga harĩ we nĩguo mamũigue.
English: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming close to him to hear him.

Kikuyu: No Afarisai na arutani a watho magĩteta makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ nĩamũkagĩra ehia na akarĩĩanĩra nao.”
English: The Pharisees and the scribes murmured, saying, “This man welcomes sinners, and eats with them.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jesũ akĩmahe ngerekano ĩno akĩmeera atĩrĩ:
English: He told them this parable:

Kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũmwe wanyu angĩkorwo na ngʼondu igana rĩmwe, na ĩmwe yacio yũre-rĩ, githĩ ndatigaga icio mĩrongo kenda na kenda kũu werũ- inĩ, athiĩ agacarie ĩyo ĩmwe yũrĩte, nginya amĩone?
English: “Which of you men, if you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them, wouldn’t leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go after the one that was lost, until he found it?

Kikuyu: Na aarĩkia kũmĩona, akamĩigĩrĩra ciande ciake akenete,
English: When he has found it, he carries it on his shoulders, rejoicing.

Kikuyu: akainũka mũciĩ. Agacooka ageeta arata ake na andũ a itũũra hamwe, akameera atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkai tũkenanĩre nĩ ũndũ nĩnyonete ngʼondu yakwa ĩrĩa ĩrorĩte.’
English: When he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!’

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũguo noguo kũgĩaga gĩkeno kĩnene igũrũ nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ ũmwe mwĩhia wĩrirĩte, gũkĩra ũrĩa gũkenagwo nĩ ũndũ wa andũ mĩrongo kenda na kenda athingu arĩa matabatarĩtio nĩ kwĩrira.
English: I tell you that even so there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents, than over ninety-nine righteous people who need no repentance.

Kikuyu: “Kana mũtumia angĩkorwo arĩ na icunjĩ ikũmi cia betha, ĩmwe yacio yũre-rĩ, githĩ ndagwatagia tawa, akahaata nyũmba yothe, na akamĩcaria na kĩyo o nginya amĩone?
English: “Or what woman, if she had ten drachma† coins, if she lost one drachma coin, wouldn’t light a lamp, sweep the house, and seek diligently until she found it?

Kikuyu: Na amĩona-rĩ, ageeta arata ake o na andũ a itũũra hamwe, akameera atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkai tũkenanĩre tondũ nĩnyonete mbeeca yakwa ĩrĩa ĩrorĩte.’
English: When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the drachma which I had lost!’

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũguo noguo gũkoragwo na gĩkeno harĩ araika a Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa mwĩhia ũmwe wĩrirĩte.”
English: Even so, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner repenting.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩthiĩ na mbere akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kwarĩ na mũndũ ũmwe warĩ na ariũ eerĩ.
English: He said, “A certain man had two sons.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa mũnini akĩĩra ithe atĩrĩ, ‘Baba ngaĩra igai rĩrĩa rĩngĩgatuĩka rĩakwa.’ Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ithe akĩmagayania indo ciake.
English: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me my share of your property.’ So he divided his livelihood between them.

Kikuyu: “Na matukũ manini mathira, mũriũ ũcio mũnini agĩcookanĩrĩria indo ciake ciothe, akiumagara agĩthiĩ bũrũri warĩ kũraya, na arĩ kũu agĩitanga indo ciake na mũtũũrĩre wa ũmaramari.
English: Not many days after, the younger son gathered all of this together and traveled into a far country. There he wasted his property with riotous living.

Kikuyu: Nake aarĩkia kũhũthĩra indo ciake ciothe, gũkĩgĩa na ngʼaragu nene kũu bũrũri ũcio wothe, nake akĩambĩrĩria gũthĩĩnĩka.
English: When he had spent all of it, there arose a severe famine in that country, and he began to be in need.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩthiĩ kũũria wĩra kũrĩ mũndũ ũmwe wa bũrũri ũcio, nake akĩmũtwara akarĩithagie ngũrwe gĩthaka-inĩ gĩake.
English: He went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed pigs.

Kikuyu: Nĩeriragĩria kwĩhũũnia na makoro marĩa maarĩĩagwo nĩ ngũrwe icio tondũ gũtirĩ mũndũ wamũheaga kĩndũ gĩa kũrĩa.
English: He wanted to fill his belly with the pods that the pigs ate, but no one gave him any.

Kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩa ecookerire, akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ ndungata ciigana atĩa cia baba irĩ na irio cia gũciigana o na igatigara, na niĩ nguagĩre gũkũ nĩ ngʼaragu!
English: But when he came to himself, he said, ‘How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough to spare, and I’m dying with hunger!

Kikuyu: Nĩngũũkĩra thiĩ kũrĩ baba ngamwĩre atĩrĩ: Baba, nĩnjĩhĩirie Ngai na ngakwĩhĩria.
English: I will get up and go to my father, and will tell him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight.

Kikuyu: Ndĩagĩrĩirwo nĩgũcooka gwĩtagwo mũrũguo rĩngĩ; ndua ta ndungata ĩmwe yaku.’
English: I am no more worthy to be called your son. Make me as one of your hired servants.” ’

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩũkĩra, agĩthiĩ kũrĩ ithe. “No arĩ o haraaya, ithe akĩmuona, nake akĩringwo nĩ tha nĩ ũndũ wake; agĩtengʼera kũrĩ mũrũwe, akĩmũhĩmbĩria na akĩmũmumunya.
English: “He arose and came to his father. But while he was still far off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion, and ran, fell on his neck, and kissed him.

Kikuyu: “Nake mũriũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Baba, nĩnjĩhĩirie Ngai o na ngakwĩhĩria. Ndiagĩrĩirwo nĩgũcooka gwĩtagwo mũrũguo rĩngĩ.’
English: The son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’

Kikuyu: “No ithe akĩĩra ndungata ciake atĩrĩ, ‘Thiĩi narua mũrehe nguo ĩrĩa njega mũno mũmũhumbe, na mũmwĩkĩre gĩcũhĩ kĩara, na iraatũ magũrũ.
English: “But the father said to his servants, ‘Bring out the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his hand and sandals on his feet.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ mũrehe njaũ ya gĩcegũ ĩrĩa noru mũmĩthĩnje. Mũreke tũgĩe na iruga na tũkũngũĩre.
English: Bring the fattened calf, kill it, and let’s eat and celebrate;

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũriũ ũyũ wakwa ararĩ mũkuũ na nĩariũkĩte; nĩarorĩte na rĩu nĩonekete.’ Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩambĩrĩria gũkũngũĩra.
English: for this, my son, was dead and is alive again. He was lost and is found.’ Then they began to celebrate.

Kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo mũriũ ũrĩa mũkũrũ aarĩ mũgũnda. Na rĩrĩa aakuhĩrĩirie mũciĩ akĩinũka-rĩ, akĩigua gũkĩinwo nyĩmbo na ndũhiũ.
English: “Now his elder son was in the field. As he came near to the house, he heard music and dancing.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũriũ ũcio agĩĩta ndungata ĩmwe ya mũciĩ, akĩmĩũria gĩtũmi kĩa maũndũ macio.
English: He called one of the servants to him and asked what was going on.

Kikuyu: Nayo ndungata ĩyo ĩkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrũ wa maitũguo nĩokĩte, nake thoguo nĩamũthĩnjĩire njaũ ya gĩcegũ ĩrĩa noru, tondũ nĩamũcookereire arĩ mwega na arĩ mũgima.’
English: He said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and healthy.’

Kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa nyina ũcio mũkũrũ akĩrakara na akĩrega gũtoonya nyũmba. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ithe akiuma nja, akĩmũthaitha atoonye thĩinĩ.
English: But he was angry and would not go in. Therefore his father came out and begged him.

Kikuyu: No akĩĩra ithe atĩrĩ, ‘Ta thikĩrĩria! Ndũire ngũrutagĩra wĩra mĩaka ĩyo yothe, na ndirĩ ndarega gũgwathĩkĩra. No rĩrĩ, ndũrĩ waahe o na koori nĩguo tũkũngũĩre na arata akwa.
English: But he answered his father, ‘Behold, these many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed a commandment of yours, but you never gave me a goat, that I might celebrate with my friends.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa mũrũguo ũyũ ũitangĩte indo ciaku na maraya ainũka, ũkamũthĩnjĩra njaũ ya gĩcegũ ĩrĩa noru!’
English: But when this your son came, who has devoured your living with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him.’

Kikuyu: “Ithe akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrũ wakwa, wee tũtũũraga nawe hĩndĩ ciothe, na indo ciothe iria ndĩ nacio nĩ ciaku.
English: “He said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.

Kikuyu: No nĩtwagĩrĩirwo gũkũngũĩra na gũcanjamũka, tondũ mũrũ wa maitũguo ũyũ ararĩ mũkuũ na rĩu nĩariũkĩte; nĩarorĩte na rĩu nĩonekete.’ ”
English: But it was appropriate to celebrate and be glad, for this, your brother, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’ ”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩĩra arutwo ake atĩrĩ: “Nĩ kwarĩ na mũndũ ũmwe warĩ gĩtonga, nake nĩ aarĩ na mũrori wa indo ciake mũrori ũcio nĩathitangirwo kũrĩ gĩtonga kĩu atĩ nĩaitangaga indo ciake.
English: He also said to his disciples, “There was a certain rich man who had a manager. An accusation was made to him that this man was wasting his possessions.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩĩta mũrori ũcio wa indo, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ũhoro ũcio ndĩraigua ũgũkoniĩ nĩ ũrĩkũ? Heana ũhoro wa ithabu rĩa ũrũgamĩrĩri waku, tondũ ndũgũcooka kũndorera indo ciakwa rĩngĩ.’
English: He called him, and said to him, ‘What is this that I hear about you? Give an accounting of your management, for you can no longer be manager.’

Kikuyu: “Nake mũrori wa indo icio akĩĩyũria na ngoro atĩrĩ, ‘Ngwĩka atĩa rĩu? Mwathi wakwa nĩekũndig- ithia wĩra. Ndirĩ na hinya mũiganu wa kũrĩma, na nĩngũconoka kũhooya.
English: “The manager said within himself, ‘What will I do, seeing that my lord is taking away the management position from me? I don’t have strength to dig. I am ashamed to beg.

Kikuyu: Rĩu nĩndamenya ũrĩa ngwĩka nĩgeetha rĩrĩa ngaatigithio wĩra gũkũ, andũ makaanyamũkagĩra kwao mĩciĩ.’
English: I know what I will do, so that when I am removed from management, they may receive me into their houses.’

Kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩĩta mũndũ o wothe warĩ na thiirĩ wa mwathi wake. Akĩũria mũndũ wa mbere atĩrĩ, ‘Wee ũrĩ na thiirĩ ũigana atĩa wa mwathi wakwa?’
English: Calling each one of his lord’s debtors to him, he said to the first, ‘How much do you owe to my lord?’

Kikuyu: “Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Ndĩ na thiirĩ wa marebe magana meerĩ ma maguta ma mĩtamaiyũ.’ “Mũrori ũcio akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Oya marũa maku ma thiirĩ ũikare thĩ narua wandĩke atĩ ũrĩ na thiirĩ wa marebe igana rĩmwe.’
English: He said, ‘A hundred batos† of oil.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and sit down quickly and write fifty.’

Kikuyu: “Agĩcooka akĩũria mũndũ wa keerĩ atĩrĩ, ‘Na we ũrĩ na thiirĩ ũigana atĩa?’ “Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Ndĩ na thiirĩ wa makũnia igana rĩmwe ma ngano.’ “Mũrori ũcio akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Oya marũa maku ma thiirĩ wandĩke ũrĩ na thiirĩ wa makũnia mĩrongo ĩnana.’
English: Then he said to another, ‘How much do you owe?’ He said, ‘A hundred cors‡ of wheat.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and write eighty.’

Kikuyu: “Mwathi ũcio nĩagathĩrĩirie mũrũgamĩrĩri ũcio wa indo ciake ũtaarĩ mwĩhokeku nĩ ũndũ wa kũgĩa na ũũgĩ. Nĩgũkorwo andũ a gũkũ thĩ nĩ oogĩ na maũndũ mao gũkĩra andũ a ũtheri.
English: “His lord commended the dishonest manager because he had done wisely, for the children of this world are, in their own generation, wiser than the children of the light.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, hũthagĩrai ũtonga wa gũkũ thĩ na gũthondeka ũrata na andũ, nĩguo rĩrĩa ũtonga ũcio ũgaathira, mũkaamũkĩrwo ciikaro-inĩ iria cia tene na tene.
English: I tell you, make for yourselves friends by means of unrighteous mammon, so that when you fail, they may receive you into the eternal tents.

Kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũngĩĩhokerwo indo nini no ehokerwo o na indo nyingĩ, na ũrĩa wothe ũtangĩĩhokeka na indo nini o na nyingĩ ndangĩĩhokerwo.
English: He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much. He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũngĩkorwo mũtarĩ ehokeku na ũtonga wa gũkũ thĩ-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩmwĩhokera ũtonga ũrĩa wa ma?
English: If therefore you have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?

Kikuyu: Ningĩ mũngĩkorwo mũtarĩ ehokeku na indo cia andũ arĩa angĩ-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩmũhe indo cianyu ene?
English: If you have not been faithful in that which is another’s, who will give you that which is your own?

Kikuyu: “Gũtirĩ ndungata ĩngĩhota gũtungatĩra aathani eerĩ. Nĩ ũndũ no ĩthũũrire ũmwe yende ũcio ũngĩ, kana ĩĩheane harĩ ũmwe, ĩnyarare ũcio ũngĩ. Mũtingĩtungatĩra Ngai na mũtungatĩre mbeeca.”
English: No servant can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other; or else he will hold to one and despise the other. You aren’t able to serve God and Mammon.”§

Kikuyu: Nao Afarisai, arĩa meendete mbeeca, rĩrĩa maiguire maũndũ macio mothe, makĩambĩrĩria gũtemba Jesũ.
English: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, also heard all these things, and they scoffed at him.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ mwĩtuuaga ega maitho-inĩ ma andũ, no Ngai nĩoĩ ngoro cianyu, nĩgũkorwo ũndũ ũrĩa ũtuagwo wa bata mũno harĩ andũ, nĩguo ũrĩ magigi maitho-inĩ ma Ngai.
English: He said to them, “You are those who justify yourselves in the sight of men, but God knows your hearts. For that which is exalted among men is an abomination in the sight of God.

Kikuyu: “Ũhoro wa Watho na ũrutani wa Anabii watũire ũhunjagio nginya hĩndĩ ya Johana ũrĩa Mũbatithania. Kuuma hĩndĩ ĩyo, Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa ũthamaki-wa-Ngai nĩ ũhunjagio, na mũndũ o wothe nĩehatagĩrĩria aũtoonye.
English: “The law and the prophets were until John. From that time the Good News of God’s Kingdom is preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũhũthũ igũrũ na thĩ gũthira, gũkĩra gaturumo karĩa kanini mũno ka Watho keherio watho-inĩ.
English: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one tiny stroke of a pen in the law to fall.

Kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũngĩtigana na mũtumia acooke ahikie mũtumia ũngĩ, ũcio nĩatharĩtie, nake mũndũ mũrũme ũrĩa ũngĩhikia mũtumia ũtiganĩte na mũthuuri-we, nĩatharĩtie.”
English: “Everyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery. He who marries one who is divorced from a husband commits adultery.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ kwarĩ mũndũ warĩ gĩtonga wehumbaga nguo cia rangi wa ndathi na cia mũthemba wa gatani ĩrĩa njega, nake nĩeekenagia o mũthenya.
English: “Now there was a certain rich man, and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, living in luxury every day.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, kĩhingo-inĩ gĩake nĩhaigagwo mũndũ wahooyaga thendi wetagwo Lazaro, waiyũrĩtwo nĩ ironda mwĩrĩ,
English: A certain beggar, named Lazarus, was taken to his gate, full of sores,

Kikuyu: nake nĩeeriragĩria kũrĩa rũitĩki rũrĩa rwagũũaga kuuma metha-inĩ ya gĩtonga kĩu. O na ngui nacio nĩciokaga na igacũna ironda ciake.
English: and desiring to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s table. Yes, even the dogs came and licked his sores.

Kikuyu: “Ihinda nĩrĩakinyire mũhooi thendi ũcio agĩkua, nao araika makĩmũkuua makĩmũtwara harĩ Iburahĩmu. Gĩtonga kĩu o nakĩo nĩgĩakuire na gĩgĩthikwo.
English: The beggar died, and he was carried away by the angels to Abraham’s bosom. The rich man also died and was buried.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩ kũu icua-inĩ, kĩna ruo rũnene, gĩgĩtiira maitho, gĩkĩona Iburahĩmu arĩ haraaya, na Lazaro arĩ hau nake.
English: In Hades,† he lifted up his eyes, being in torment, and saw Abraham far off, and Lazarus at his bosom.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio gĩtonga gĩkĩmwĩta gĩkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Iburahĩmu, Baba, njiguĩra tha, ũtũme Lazaro atobokie mũthia wa kĩara gĩake maaĩ-inĩ ooke aahehie rũrĩmĩ, tondũ ndĩ na ruo rũnene gũkũ mwaki-inĩ ũyũ.’
English: He cried and said, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue! For I am in anguish in this flame.’

Kikuyu: “Nowe Iburahĩmu akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrũ wakwa, ririkana rĩrĩa watũire muoyo woonaga o maũndũ mega, hĩndĩ ĩyo Lazaro nake oonaga o maũndũ mooru, no rĩu we nĩmũnyamarũre, nawe ũrĩ ruo-inĩ.
English: “But Abraham said, ‘Son, remember that you, in your lifetime, received your good things, and Lazarus, in the same way, bad things. But here he is now comforted and you are in anguish.

Kikuyu: Hamwe na ũguo-rĩ, gatagatĩ gaitũ nawe harĩ na mũkuru mũriku mũno wĩkĩrĩtwo, nĩguo arĩa mangĩenda kuuma gũkũ tũrĩ moke kũrĩ we matikaahote, o na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngiuma kũu akĩre ooke gũkũ tũrĩ.’
English: Besides all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed, that those who want to pass from here to you are not able, and that no one may cross over from there to us.’

Kikuyu: “Nakĩo gĩtonga kĩu gĩgĩcookia atĩrĩ, ‘Ndagĩgũthaitha, baba, ũtũme Lazaro kũrĩ andũ a nyũmba ya baba,
English: “He said, ‘I ask you therefore, father, that you would send him to my father’s house—

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ndĩ na ariũ a baba atano. Reke athiĩ akamahe ũhoro, nĩguo matikanooke gũkũ kũrĩ na ruo rũnene ũũ.’
English: for I have five brothers—that he may testify to them, so they won’t also come into this place of torment.’

Kikuyu: “Iburahĩmu akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Marĩ na Musa na Anabii; nĩmamathikĩrĩrie.’
English: “But Abraham said to him, ‘They have Moses and the prophets. Let them listen to them.’

Kikuyu: “Nakĩo gĩtonga gĩkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Iburahĩmu, baba, acio ti aiganu; mũndũ angĩthiĩ kũrĩ o oimĩte kũrĩ arĩa akuũ, no merire.’
English: “He said, ‘No, father Abraham, but if one goes to them from the dead, they will repent.’

Kikuyu: “Agĩkĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Angĩkorwo matingĩigua Musa na Anabii, o na mũndũ angĩriũka kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ matingĩmũigua.’ ”
English: “He said to him, ‘If they don’t listen to Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded if one rises from the dead.’ ”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩĩra arutwo ake atĩrĩ, “Maũndũ marĩa matũmaga andũ mehie no nginya moke, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũtũmaga maũndũ macio moke kaĩ arĩ na haaro-ĩ!
English: He said to the disciples, “It is impossible that no occasions of stumbling should come, but woe to him through whom they come!

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba mũndũ ta ũcio ohererwo ihiga rĩa gĩthĩi ngingo aikio iria-inĩ, gũkĩra atũme kamwe ga twana tũtũ tũnini keehie.
English: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck, and he were thrown into the sea, rather than that he should cause one of these little ones to stumble.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, ikaragai mwĩiguĩte. “Mũrũ wa thoguo kana mwarĩ wa thoguo angĩĩhia, mũkaanie, na angĩĩrira-rĩ, mũrekere.
English: Be careful. If your brother sins against you, rebuke him. If he repents, forgive him.

Kikuyu: Angĩkwĩhĩria maita mũgwanja o mũthenya ũmwe, na maita macio mothe mũgwanja agooka harĩ we agakwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩndahera,’ nĩ wega ũmũrekere.”
English: If he sins against you seven times in the day, and seven times returns, saying, ‘I repent,’ you shall forgive him.”

Kikuyu: Nao atũmwo makĩĩra Mwathani atĩrĩ, “Tuongerere wĩtĩkio!”
English: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũngĩkorwo na wĩtĩkio o mũnini ta kabegũ ga karatarĩ-rĩ, mwera mũkũyũ ũyũ atĩrĩ, ‘Kũũrũka, ũkehaande iria-inĩ,’ naguo no ũmwathĩkĩre.
English: The Lord said, “If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed, you would tell this sycamore tree, ‘Be uprooted and be planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩkũ wanyu ũngĩkorwo arĩ na ndungata ya kũmũrĩmĩra na mũraũ kana ya kũmũrĩithĩria ngʼondu, na ndungata ĩyo yoimĩra mũgũnda amĩĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Ũka ũikare thĩ ũrĩe?’
English: But who is there among you, having a servant plowing or keeping sheep, that will say when he comes in from the field, ‘Come immediately and sit down at the table’?

Kikuyu: Githĩ to amĩĩrire atĩrĩ, ‘Thondekera irio cia hwaĩ-inĩ, na wĩhaarĩrie ũnjikarie ngĩrĩa na ngĩnyua; na thuutha ũcio no ũrĩe na ũnyue’?
English: Wouldn’t he rather tell him, ‘Prepare my supper, clothe yourself properly, and serve me while I eat and drink. Afterward you shall eat and drink’?

Kikuyu: Ningĩ no agĩcookerie ndungata ĩyo ngaatho nĩ gwĩka ũrĩa ĩĩrĩtwo ĩĩke?
English: Does he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded? I think not.

Kikuyu: Ũguo noguo o na inyuĩ, rĩrĩa mweka ũrĩa wothe mwĩrĩtwo mwĩke, ugagai atĩrĩ, ‘Tũrĩ o ndungata itarĩ kĩene; twaruta o wĩra witũ tu.’ ”
English: Even so you also, when you have done all the things that are commanded you, say, ‘We are unworthy servants. We have done our duty.’ ”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Jesũ arĩ rũgendo-inĩ rwa gũthiĩ Jerusalemu nĩagereire mũhaka-inĩ wa Samaria na Galili.
English: As he was on his way to Jerusalem, he was passing along the borders of Samaria and Galilee.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aatoonyaga gatũũra kamwe, agĩtũngwo nĩ andũ ikũmi maarĩ na mũrimũ wa mangũ. Makĩrũgama o haraaya,
English: As he entered into a certain village, ten men who were lepers met him, who stood at a distance.

Kikuyu: makĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo mũnene, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Jesũ, Mwathi witũ, tũiguĩre tha!”
English: They lifted up their voices, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aamoonire, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi mũkeyonanie kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai.” Na rĩrĩa maathiiaga-rĩ, magĩtherio.
English: When he saw them, he said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” As they went, they were cleansed.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ũmwe wao onire nĩahonio, akĩhũndũka akĩgooca Ngai na mũgambo mũnene,
English: One of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, glorifying God with a loud voice.

Kikuyu: Akĩĩgũithia thĩ magũrũ-inĩ ma Jesũ, akĩmũcookeria ngaatho. Mũndũ ũcio aarĩ Mũsamaria.
English: He fell on his face at Jesus’ feet, giving him thanks; and he was a Samaritan.

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Githĩ othe ikũmi matiatherio? Acio angĩ kenda marĩ ha?
English: Jesus answered, “Weren’t the ten cleansed? But where are the nine?

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ o na ũmwe ũngĩcooka kũhe Ngai ũgooci tiga o mũgeni ũyũ?”
English: Were there none found who returned to give glory to God, except this foreigner?”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩra wĩthiĩre; gwĩtĩkia gwaku nĩkuo gwatũma ũhone.”
English: Then he said to him, “Get up, and go your way. Your faith has healed you.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ĩmwe Afarisai nĩmoririe Jesũ rĩrĩa ũthamaki wa Ngai ũgooka. Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũthamaki wa Ngai ndũũkaga na imenyithia iria mũngĩĩrorera,
English: Being asked by the Pharisees when God’s Kingdom would come, he answered them, “God’s Kingdom doesn’t come with observation;

Kikuyu: kana andũ moige atĩrĩ, ‘Ũrĩ haha,’ kana ‘Ũrĩ harĩa,’ tondũ ũthamaki wa Ngai ũrĩ thĩinĩ wanyu.”
English: neither will they say, ‘Look, here!’ or, ‘Look, there!’ for behold, God’s Kingdom is within you.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩĩra arutwo ake atĩrĩ, “Ihinda nĩrĩroka rĩrĩa mũkeriragĩria kuona mũthenya ũmwe wa Mũrũ wa Mũndũ, no mũtikawona.
English: He said to the disciples, “The days will come when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.

Kikuyu: Andũ nĩmakamwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Arĩ harĩa!’ kana ‘Arĩ haha!’ Mũtikanatengʼere mũmarũmĩrĩre.
English: They will tell you, ‘Look, here!’ or ‘Look, there!’ Don’t go away or follow after them,

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa rũheni rũhenũkaga rũkamũrĩka matu kuuma mũthia ũmwe nginya ũrĩa ũngĩ, no taguo gũũka kwa Mũrũ wa Mũndũ gũkahaana mũthenya ũcio wake.
English: for as the lightning, when it flashes out of one part under the sky, shines to another part under the sky, so will the Son of Man be in his day.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, no nginya ambe one mathĩĩna maingĩ na aregwo nĩ andũ a rũciaro rũrũ.
English: But first, he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.

Kikuyu: “O ta ũrĩa gwatariĩ matukũ-inĩ ma Nuhu, noguo gũkaahaana matukũ-inĩ ma Mũrũ wa Mũndũ.
English: As it was in the days of Noah, even so it will also be in the days of the Son of Man.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio maakorirwo makĩrĩa, na makĩnyua, na makĩhikania, na makĩhikio, o nginya mũthenya ũrĩa Nuhu aatoonyire thabina. Hĩndĩ ĩyo kĩguũ kĩnene gĩgĩũka gĩkĩmaniina othe.
English: They ate, they drank, they married, and they were given in marriage until the day that Noah entered into the ship, and the flood came and destroyed them all.

Kikuyu: “Ningĩ ũguo noguo gwatariĩ matukũ-inĩ ma Loti. Andũ acio maakorirwo makĩrĩa na makĩnyua, na makĩgũra na makĩendia, na makĩhaanda na magĩaka nyũmba.
English: Likewise, even as it was in the days of Lot: they ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they built;

Kikuyu: No mũthenya ũrĩa Loti oimire Sodomu, mwaki na ũbiriti ikiura ta mbura kuuma igũrũ, ikĩmaniina othe.
English: but in the day that Lot went out from Sodom, it rained fire and sulfur from the sky and destroyed them all.

Kikuyu: “Ũguo noguo gũkahaana mũthenya ũrĩa Mũrũ wa Mũndũ akaaguũranĩrio.
English: It will be the same way in the day that the Son of Man is revealed.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio, mũndũ o wothe ũgaakorwo arĩ nyũmba igũrũ, na indo ciake irĩ nyũmba thĩinĩ, ndakanaharũrũke gũcigĩĩra. Ningĩ o na mũndũ ũrĩa ũgaakorwo arĩ mũgũnda ndakanahũndũke agĩĩre kĩndũ.
English: In that day, he who will be on the housetop and his goods in the house, let him not go down to take them away. Let him who is in the field likewise not turn back.

Kikuyu: Ta ririkanai mũtumia wa Loti!
English: Remember Lot’s wife!

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũgeragia gũtũũria muoyo wake nĩakoorwo nĩguo, nake ũrĩa wothe ũũragwo nĩ muoyo wake nĩakaũtũũria.
English: Whoever seeks to save his life loses it, but whoever loses his life preserves it.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũtukũ ũcio andũ eerĩ nĩmagakorwo makomete ũrĩrĩ ũmwe, nake ũmwe wao akuuo, na ũrĩa ũngĩ atigwo.
English: I tell you, in that night there will be two people in one bed. One will be taken and the other will be left.

Kikuyu: Atumia eerĩ nĩmagakorwo magĩthĩa hamwe; ũmwe wao akuuo, na ũrĩa ũngĩ atigwo.
English: There will be two grinding grain together. One will be taken and the other will be left.”

Kikuyu: (Ningĩ arũme eerĩ nĩmagakorwo makĩruta wĩra hamwe, ũmwe wao akuuo, ũrĩa ũngĩ atigwo.)”
Nao makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Kũu nĩ kũ Mwathani?” Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Nderi ciũnganaga harĩa hothe harĩ kĩimba.”
English: They, answering, asked him, “Where, Lord?” He said to them, “Where the body is, there the vultures will also be gathered together.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩhe arutwo ake ngerekano ya kũmaruta atĩ nĩmagĩrĩirwo nĩkũhooyaga hĩndĩ ciothe mategũkua ngoro.
English: He also spoke a parable to them that they must always pray and not give up,

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Itũũra-inĩ rĩmwe nĩ kwarĩ na mũtuanĩri ciira ũteetigagĩra Ngai kana agatĩĩa andũ.
English: saying, “There was a judge in a certain city who didn’t fear God and didn’t respect man.

Kikuyu: Na itũũra-inĩ rĩu nĩ kwarĩ mũtumia ũmwe wa ndigwa wathiiaga kũrĩ we kaingĩ akamwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ndĩhĩria harĩ thũ ciakwa.’
English: A widow was in that city, and she often came to him, saying, ‘Defend me from my adversary!’

Kikuyu: “No mũtuanĩri ciira ũcio akĩrega kũmũteithia kwa ihinda. No marigĩrĩrio akĩĩra na ngoro atĩrĩ, ‘O na gũtuĩka ndiĩtigĩrĩte Ngai kana ngatĩĩa andũ-rĩ,
English: He wouldn’t for a while; but afterward he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God nor respect man,

Kikuyu: no tondũ wa ũrĩa mũtumia ũyũ wa ndigwa atiindaga agĩĩthĩĩnia, nĩngũtigĩrĩra ciira wake nĩwatuuo na kĩhooto nĩguo atige kũũnogagia na gũtindaga agĩũka!’ ”
English: yet because this widow bothers me, I will defend her, or else she will wear me out by her continual coming.’ ”

Kikuyu: Nake Mwathani akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩmwaigua ũrĩa mũtuanĩri ciira ũcio ũtarĩ kĩhooto oigire?
English: The Lord said, “Listen to what the unrighteous judge says.

Kikuyu: Githĩ o nake Ngai ndagatua ciira na kĩhooto wa arĩa ake ethuurĩire, o arĩa mamũkayagĩra mũthenya na ũtukũ? No aage kũmateithia na ihenya?
English: Won’t God avenge his chosen ones who are crying out to him day and night, and yet he exercises patience with them?

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, agaatigĩrĩra atĩ nĩmaciirĩrwo na kĩhooto, o na ningĩ na ihenya. O na kũrĩ ũguo-rĩ, rĩrĩa Mũrũ wa Mũndũ agooka-rĩ, nĩagakora andũ metĩkĩtie gũkũ thĩ?”
English: I tell you that he will avenge them quickly. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jesũ akĩarĩria andũ amwe arĩa meĩhokaga o ene, magetuaga athingu na makahũthagia andũ arĩa angĩ othe, akĩmahe ngerekano, akĩmeera atĩrĩ:
English: He also spoke this parable to certain people who were convinced of their own righteousness, and who despised all others:

Kikuyu: “Andũ eerĩ nĩmambatire hekarũ-inĩ kũhooya. Ũmwe aarĩ Mũfarisai na ũcio ũngĩ aarĩ mwĩtia wa mbeeca cia igooti.
English: “Two men went up into the temple to pray; one was a Pharisee, and the other was a tax collector.

Kikuyu: Mũfarisai ũcio akĩrũgama akĩĩhooera we mwene, akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Ngai, nĩndagũcookeria ngaatho nĩgũkorwo ndihaana ta andũ arĩa angĩ, arĩa atunyani, na arĩa mekaga ũũru, na itharia, o na kana ta mwĩtia ũyũ wa mbeeca cia igooti.
English: The Pharisee stood and prayed by himself like this: ‘God, I thank you that I am not like the rest of men: extortionists, unrighteous, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩĩhingaga kũrĩa irio maita meerĩ o kiumia, na ngaruta gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kĩa indo ciothe iria nyonaga.’
English: I fast twice a week. I give tithes of all that I get.’

Kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, mwĩtia wa mbeeca cia igooti ũcio akĩrũgama o haraaya; na ndaigana gũtiira maitho make na igũrũ, no nĩ kwĩgũtha eegũthaga gĩthũri enyiihĩtie, akoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Ngai, njiguĩra tha, niĩ mũndũ mwĩhia.’
English: But the tax collector, standing far away, wouldn’t even lift up his eyes to heaven, but beat his chest, saying, ‘God, be merciful to me, a sinner!’

Kikuyu: “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, mwĩtia mbeeca cia igooti nĩainũkire atuĩtwo mũthingu mbere ya Ngai gũkĩra ũcio ũngĩ. Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa wothe wĩtũgagĩria nĩakanyiihio nake ũrĩa wĩnyiihagia nĩagatũũgĩrio.”
English: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but he who humbles himself will be exalted.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, andũ nao nĩmarehagĩra Jesũ twana nĩguo atũhutie. Arutwo ake mona ũguo, makĩ- makaania.
English: They were also bringing their babies to him, that he might touch them. But when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.

Kikuyu: Nowe Jesũ agĩĩta twana tũu harĩ we, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Rekei twana tũu tũũke kũrĩ niĩ, tigai gũtũgiria, nĩgũkorwo ũthamaki wa Ngai nĩ wa arĩa matariĩ ta tuo.
English: Jesus summoned them, saying, “Allow the little children to come to me, and don’t hinder them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũrĩa wothe ũtakamũkĩra ũthamaki wa Ngai ta kaana ndakaũtoonya.”
English: Most certainly, I tell you, whoever doesn’t receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.”

Kikuyu: Mũnene ũmwe akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani mwega, ingĩĩka atĩa nĩguo ngaagaya muoyo wa tene na tene?”
English: A certain ruler asked him, saying, “Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũkũnjĩta mwega nĩkĩ? Gũtirĩ o na ũmwe mwega tiga Ngai we wiki.
English: Jesus asked him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good, except one: God.

Kikuyu: We nĩũĩ ũrĩa maathani moigĩte, ‘Ndũkanatharanie, na ndũkanoragane, na ndũkanaiye, na ndũkanaigĩrĩre mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ kĩgeenyo, na tĩĩa thoguo na nyũkwa.’ ”
English: You know the commandments: ‘Don’t commit adultery,’ ‘Don’t murder,’ ‘Don’t steal,’ ‘Don’t give false testimony,’ ‘Honor your father and your mother.’ ”*

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Maathani macio mothe ndũire ndĩmarũmĩtie kuuma ndĩ o mũnini.”
English: He said, “I have observed all these things from my youth up.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ aigua ũguo, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Harĩ ũndũ ũmwe ũtigarĩtie gwĩka. Thiĩ wendie kĩrĩa gĩothe ũrĩ nakĩo ũhe athĩĩni mbeeca icio, na nĩũkũgĩa na mũthiithũ igũrũ. Ũcooke ũũke ũnũmĩrĩre.”
English: When Jesus heard these things, he said to him, “You still lack one thing. Sell all that you have and distribute it to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũnene ũcio aiguire ũguo, akĩigua kĩeha mũno, tondũ aarĩ mũtongu mũno.
English: But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was very rich.

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmũrora, akiugaatĩrĩ, “Kaĩnĩhinyaandũarĩaatongugũtoonyaũthamaki-inĩwaNgai-ĩ!
English: Jesus, seeing that he became very sad, said, “How hard it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!

Kikuyu: Nĩũhũthũ ngamĩĩra ĩhungurĩre irima rĩa cindano, gũkĩra gĩtonga gũtoonya ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai.”
English: For it is easier for a camel to enter in through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”

Kikuyu: Nao arĩa maiguire ũhoro ũcio makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũngĩkĩhota kũhonoka?”
English: Those who heard it said, “Then who can be saved?”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Maũndũ marĩa matahotekaga nĩ andũ nĩmahotekaga nĩ Ngai.”
English: But he said, “The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.”

Kikuyu: Nake Petero akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtigĩte maũndũ maitũ mothe tũgakũrũmĩrĩra!”
English: Peter said, “Look, we have left everything and followed you.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmeera, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, gũtirĩ mũndũ ũtigĩte mũciĩ wake, kana mũtumia wake, kana ariũ a nyina, kana aciari ake, o na kana ciana ciake, nĩ ũndũ wa ũthamaki wa Ngai,
English: He said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or wife, or brothers, or parents, or children, for God’s Kingdom’s sake,

Kikuyu: ũtakaheo maita maingĩ ma kĩrĩa atigĩte ihinda-inĩ rĩĩrĩ tũrĩ, na ihinda rĩrĩa rĩgooka aheo muoyo wa tene na tene.”
English: who will not receive many times more in this time, and in the world to come, eternal life.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Jesũ agĩtwara arutwo ake arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ handũ keheri-inĩ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũk- wambata tũthiĩ Jerusalemu, na maũndũ mothe marĩa maandĩkirwo nĩ anabii makoniĩ Mũrũ wa Mũndũ no nginya mahingio.
English: He took the twelve aside and said to them, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all the things that are written through the prophets concerning the Son of Man will be completed.

Kikuyu: Nake nĩakaneanwo kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ. Nao nĩ makaamũnyũrũria, na mamũrume, na mamũtuĩre mata, na mamũhũũre iboko, o na mamũũrage.
English: For he will be delivered up to the Gentiles, will be mocked, treated shamefully, and spit on.

Kikuyu: Naguo mũthenya wa gatatũ nĩakariũka.”
English: They will scourge and kill him. On the third day, he will rise again.”

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo ake makĩaga kũmenya ũndũ o na ũmwe wa macio. Nĩmahithĩtwo ũhoro ũcio na matiamenyire ũrĩa aameeraga.
English: They understood none of these things. This saying was hidden from them, and they didn’t understand the things that were said.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akuhĩrĩria Jeriko, mũndũ warĩ mũtumumu nĩaikarĩte mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra akĩhooya mbeeca.
English: As he came near Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the road, begging.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aiguire andũ aingĩ makĩhĩtũka, akĩũria ũrĩa gwekĩkaga.
English: Hearing a multitude going by, he asked what this meant.

Kikuyu: Andũ makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ Jesũ wa Nazarethi ũrahĩtũka.”
English: They told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩanĩrĩra, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Jesũ, mũrũ wa Daudi, njiguĩra tha.”
English: He cried out, “Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!”

Kikuyu: Arĩa maatongoretie makĩmũkaania makĩmwĩra akire, nowe agĩkĩrĩrĩria kwanĩrĩra akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa Daudi, njiguĩra tha!”
English: Those who led the way rebuked him, that he should be quiet; but he cried out all the more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩrũgama, agĩathana, mũndũ ũcio atwarwo harĩ we.
English: Standing still, Jesus commanded him to be brought to him. When he had come near, he asked him,

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aakuhĩrĩirie, Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũkwenda ngwĩkĩre atĩa?” Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, ngwenda njooke kuona.”
English: “What do you want me to do?” He said, “Lord, that I may see again.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Cooka kuona, gwĩtĩkia gwaku nĩkuo gwatũma ũhone.”
English: Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight. Your faith has healed you.”

Kikuyu: O rĩmwe akĩambĩrĩria kuona, nake akĩrũmĩrĩra Jesũ, akĩgoocaga Ngai. Rĩrĩa andũ othe moonire ũguo, o nao makĩgooca Ngai.
English: Immediately he received his sight and followed him, glorifying God. All the people, when they saw it, praised God.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Jesũ nĩatoonyire Jeriko, akĩhĩtũkĩra kuo.
English: He entered and was passing through Jericho.

Kikuyu: Nakuo kũu Jeriko nĩ kwarĩ mũndũ wetagwo Zakayo; warĩ mũnene wa etia mbeeca cia igooti na aarĩ gĩtonga.
English: There was a man named Zacchaeus. He was a chief tax collector, and he was rich.

Kikuyu: Nĩendaga kuona Jesũ aarĩ ũ, no ndangĩahotire kũmuona nĩ ũndũ wa ũingĩ wa andũ, na tondũ aarĩ mũndũ mũkuhĩ.
English: He was trying to see who Jesus was, and couldn’t because of the crowd, because he was short.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩhanyũka mbere yao, akĩhaica mũkũyũ nĩguo amuone, nĩgũkorwo Jesũ aageragĩra njĩra ĩyo.
English: He ran on ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree to see him, for he was going to pass that way.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ aakinya hau, agĩtiira maitho na igũrũ, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Zakayo, harũrũka narua, tondũ ũmũthĩ no nginya thiĩ gwaku mũciĩ.”
English: When Jesus came to the place, he looked up and saw him, and said to him, “Zacchaeus, hurry and come down, for today I must stay at your house.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩharũrũka narua, akĩmũnyiita ũgeni akenete.
English: He hurried, came down, and received him joyfully.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ moona ũguo makĩambĩrĩria kũnuguna, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ aathiĩ gũceera kwa mũndũ mwĩhia.”
English: When they saw it, they all murmured, saying, “He has gone in to lodge with a man who is a sinner.”

Kikuyu: Nowe Zakayo akĩrũgama, akĩĩra Mwathani atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, rĩu nĩngũheana nuthu ya indo ciakwa kũrĩ athĩĩni, na hangĩkorwo harĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ndunyĩte kĩndũ, nĩngũmũrĩha kĩndũ kĩu maita mana.”
English: Zacchaeus stood and said to the Lord, “Behold, Lord, half of my goods I give to the poor. If I have wrongfully exacted anything of anyone, I restore four times as much.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũmũthĩ ũhonokio nĩũkinyĩte nyũmba ĩno, tondũ mũndũ ũyũ o nake nĩ mũrũ wa Iburahĩmu.
English: Jesus said to him, “Today, salvation has come to this house, because he also is a son of Abraham.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Mũrũ wa Mũndũ ookire gũcaria na kũhonokia kĩrĩa kĩũrĩte.”
English: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was lost.”

Kikuyu: Na o mathikĩrĩirie maũndũ macio, agĩthiĩ na mbere kũmahe ũhoro na ngerekano, tondũ nĩakuhĩrĩirie Jerusalemu, na andũ acio meciiragia atĩ ũthamaki wa Ngai warĩ o hakuhĩ gũkinya.
English: As they heard these things, he went on and told a parable, because he was near Jerusalem, and they supposed that God’s Kingdom would be revealed immediately.

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Mũndũ ũmwe warĩ igweta nĩathiire bũrũri wa kũraya agatuuo mũthamaki, na thuutha wa ũguo acooke.
English: He said therefore, “A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom and to return.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩĩta ndungata ciake ikũmi, agĩcihe mina ikũmi, agĩciĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Onjorithagiai mbeeca ici nginya rĩrĩa ngacooka.’
English: He called ten servants of his and gave them ten mina coins, † and told them, ‘Conduct business until I come.’

Kikuyu: “No andũ a bũrũri wake nĩmamũthũire, nao magĩtũma andũ mamuume thuutha makoige atĩrĩ, ‘Tũtikwenda mũndũ ũyũ atuĩke mũthamaki witũ.’
English: But his citizens hated him, and sent an envoy after him, saying, ‘We don’t want this man to reign over us.’

Kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa aacookire arĩkĩtie gũtuuo mũthamaki, agĩtũmanĩra ndungata iria aaneete mbeeca, nĩguo amenye uumithio ũrĩa cionete.
English: “When he had come back again, having received the kingdom, he commanded these servants, to whom he had given the money, to be called to him, that he might know what they had gained by conducting business.

Kikuyu: “Ndungata ya mbere ĩgĩũka, ĩkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathi, mina yaku nĩyumithĩtie mina ingĩ ikũmi.’
English: The first came before him, saying, ‘Lord, your mina has made ten more minas.’

Kikuyu: “Nake Mwathi wayo akĩmĩcookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩwĩkĩte wega, wee ndungata yakwa njega! Na tondũ wa kwĩhokeka na ũndũ mũnini ũguo, tuĩka mũrori wa matũũra manene ikũmi.’
English: “He said to him, ‘Well done, you good servant! Because you were found faithful with very little, you shall have authority over ten cities.’

Kikuyu: “Nayo ndungata ya keerĩ ĩgĩũka, ĩkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathi, mina yaku nĩyumithĩtie mina ingĩ ithano.’
English: “The second came, saying, ‘Your mina, Lord, has made five minas.’

Kikuyu: “Nake mwathi wayo akĩmĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Tuĩka mũrori wa matũũra manene matano.’
English: “So he said to him, ‘And you are to be over five cities.’

Kikuyu: “Nayo, ndungata ya gatatũ ĩgĩũka, ĩkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathi, mina yaku nĩyo ĩno; ndaamĩohire na gĩtambaya ngĩmĩhitha.
English: Another came, saying, ‘Lord, behold, your mina, which I kept laid away in a handkerchief,

Kikuyu: Nĩndagwĩtigĩrire, tondũ ũrĩ mũndũ mũũru. Nĩwoyaga kĩrĩa ũtarutĩire wĩra, na ũkagetha kĩrĩa ũtahandĩte.’
English: for I feared you, because you are an exacting man. You take up that which you didn’t lay down, and reap that which you didn’t sow.’

Kikuyu: “Nake mwathi wayo akĩmĩcookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Wee ndungata ĩno njaganu! Ngũgũtuĩra ciira kũringana na ciugo ciaku. Githĩ nĩwooĩ atĩ ndĩ mũũru, na atĩ njoyaga kĩrĩa itarutĩire wĩra na ngagetha kĩrĩa itahaandĩte?
English: “He said to him, ‘Out of your own mouth I will judge you, you wicked servant! You knew that I am an exacting man, taking up that which I didn’t lay down and reaping that which I didn’t sow.

Kikuyu: Wakĩagire kũiga mbeeca ciakwa bengi nĩkĩ, nĩgeetha rĩrĩa ngaacooka ndĩcioye irĩ na uumithio?’
English: Then why didn’t you deposit my money in the bank, and at my coming, I might have earned interest on it?’

Kikuyu: “Agĩcooka akĩĩra arĩa maarũgamĩte hau atĩrĩ, ‘Mũtunyei mina ĩyo arĩ nayo, mũmĩnengere ũrĩa ũrĩ na mina ikũmi.’
English: He said to those who stood by, ‘Take the mina away from him and give it to him who has the ten minas.’

Kikuyu: “Makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathi, githĩ ndarĩ na mina ikũmi!’
English: “They said to him, ‘Lord, he has ten minas!’

Kikuyu: “Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũrĩ na kĩndũ, nĩakongererwo ingĩ nyingĩ, no ũrĩa ũtarĩ na kĩndũ, o na kĩrĩa arĩ nakĩo nĩagatuunywo.
English: ‘For I tell you that to everyone who has, will more be given; but from him who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, andũ acio marĩ ũthũ na niĩ, o acio mateendaga nduĩke mũthamaki wao-rĩ, marehei, mũmooragĩre o haha ndĩ.’ ”
English: But bring those enemies of mine who didn’t want me to reign over them here, and kill them before me.’ ”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, agĩthiĩ na mbere kwambata erekeire Jerusalemu.
English: Having said these things, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Aakuhĩrĩria Bethifage na Bethania, o kũu Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Mĩtamaiyũ, agĩtũma arutwo ake eerĩ, akĩmeera atĩrĩ,
English: When he came near to Bethsphage‡ and Bethany, at the mountain that is called Olivet, he sent two of his disciples,

Kikuyu: “Thiĩi itũũra rĩrĩa rĩrĩ mbere yanyu, na mũgĩtoonya, nĩmũkuona njaũ ya ndigiri yohetwo hau, nayo ndĩrĩ yakuua mũndũ; mĩohorei mũmĩrehe.
English: saying, “Go your way into the village on the other side, in which, as you enter, you will find a colt tied, which no man has ever sat upon. Untie it and bring it.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩmũũria atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ kĩĩ kĩratũma mũmĩohore?’ Mwĩrei atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani nĩabatarĩtio nĩyo.’ ”
English: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you untying it?’ say to him: ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”

Kikuyu: Nao acio maatũmirwo magĩthiĩ, magĩkora maũndũ matariĩ o ta ũrĩa Jesũ aamerĩte.
English: Those who were sent went away and found things just as he had told them.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa moohoraga njaũ ĩyo, eene yo makĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũroohora njaũ ĩyo nĩkĩ?”
English: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, “Why are you untying the colt?”

Kikuyu: Nao magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Mwathani nĩabatarĩtio nĩyo.”
English: They said, “The Lord needs it.”

Kikuyu: Makĩmĩtwarĩra Jesũ, magĩcooka makĩigĩrĩra nguo ciao igũrũ rĩa njaũ ĩyo, na magĩikarĩria Jesũ igũrũ rĩayo.
English: Then they brought it to Jesus. They threw their cloaks on the colt and sat Jesus on them.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aathiiaga, andũ makĩara nguo ciao njĩra-inĩ.
English: As he went, they spread their cloaks on the road.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aakinyire hakuhĩ na njĩra ĩrĩa ĩikũrũkagĩra Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Mĩtamaiyũ, gĩkundi gĩothe kĩa arutwo gĩkĩambĩrĩria kũgooca Ngai, andũ makĩanĩrĩra makenete nĩ ũndũ wa ciama iria ciothe moonete makiuga atĩrĩ:
English: As he was now getting near, at the descent of the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works which they had seen,

Kikuyu: “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũthamaki ũrĩa ũgũũka na rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani!” “Thayũ ũrogĩa kũu igũrũ, naguo riiri ũgĩe kũu igũrũ mũno!”
English: saying, “Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord!* Peace in heaven, and glory in the highest!”

Kikuyu: Afarisai amwe maarĩ gĩkundi-inĩ kĩu makĩĩra Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, kaania arutwo aku!”
English: Some of the Pharisees from the multitude said to him, “Teacher, rebuke your disciples!”

Kikuyu: Nowe akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, aya maangĩkira, mahiga no maanĩrĩre.”
English: He answered them, “I tell you that if these were silent, the stones would cry out.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aakuhĩrĩirie itũũra rĩu inene rĩa Jerusalemu, aarĩona akĩrĩra nĩ ũndũ warĩo,
English: When he came near, he saw the city and wept over it,

Kikuyu: akiuga atĩrĩ, “Naarĩ korwo ũmũthĩ wee mwene nĩũkũmenyete kĩrĩa kĩngĩkũrehere thayũ! No rĩu, ũhoro ũcio nĩũhithĩtwo maitho maku.
English: saying, “If you, even you, had known today the things which belong to your peace! But now, they are hidden from your eyes.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ nĩĩgakinya rĩrĩa thũ ciaku igaaka ihumbu cia tĩĩri ikũrigiicĩrie, na ikũhingĩrĩrie na mĩena yothe.
English: For the days will come on you when your enemies will throw up a barricade against you, surround you, hem you in on every side,

Kikuyu: Nĩigagũtungumania thĩ, wee hamwe na ciana ciaku iria irĩ kũu thĩinĩ waku. Matigatiga ihiga o na rĩmwe rĩrĩ igũrũ wa rĩrĩa rĩngĩ, tondũ ndũigana gũkũũrana ihinda rĩrĩa Ngai ookire gũgũceerera.”
English: and will dash you and your children within you to the ground. They will not leave in you one stone on another, because you didn’t know the time of your visitation.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩtoonya hekarũ thĩinĩ, na akĩambĩrĩria kũrutũrũra andũ arĩa meendagĩria indo kuo.
English: He entered into the temple and began to drive out those who bought and sold in it,

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ, ‘Nyũmba yakwa ĩgeetagwo nyũmba ya kũhooyagĩrwo’ no inyuĩ mũmĩtuĩte ‘ngurunga ya atunyani.’ ”
English: saying to them, “It is written, ‘My house is a house of prayer,’ * but you have made it a ‘den of robbers’!” *

Kikuyu: Na o mũthenya, nĩarutanaga kũu thĩinĩ wa hekarũ. No athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na arutani a watho, na atongoria arĩa maarĩ gatagatĩ ka andũ nĩmageragia kũmũũraga.
English: He was teaching daily in the temple, but the chief priests, the scribes, and the leading men among the people sought to destroy him.

Kikuyu: No matingĩonire njĩra ya kũmũũraga, tondũ andũ othe nĩmaikaraga mathikĩrĩirie ciugo ciake.
English: They couldn’t find what they might do, for all the people hung on to every word that he said.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya ũmwe akĩruta andũ kũu hekarũ-inĩ na akĩhunjagia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega-rĩ, athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na arutani a watho, hamwe na athuuri magĩũka kũrĩ we.
English: On one of those days, as he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the Good News, the† priests and scribes came to him with the elders.

Kikuyu: Makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Twĩre wĩkaga maũndũ maya na ũhoti ũrĩkũ. Nũũ wakũheire ũhoti ũcio?”
English: They asked him, “Tell us: by what authority do you do these things? Or who is giving you this authority?”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “O na niĩ nĩngũmũũria kĩũria.
English: He answered them, “I also will ask you one question. Tell me:

Kikuyu: Ta njĩĩrai, ũbatithio wa Johana-rĩ, woimĩte igũrũ kana woimire kũrĩ andũ?”
English: the baptism of John, was it from heaven, or from men?”

Kikuyu: Makĩaranĩria o ene ũhoro ũcio, makĩĩrana atĩrĩ, “Tũngiuga atĩ ‘Woimĩte igũrũ’ egũtũũria atĩrĩ, ‘Mwakĩregire kũmwĩtĩkia nĩkĩ?’
English: They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why didn’t you believe him?’

Kikuyu: Ningĩ tuoiga atĩ ‘Woimĩte kũrĩ andũ’, andũ othe nĩmegũtũhũũra na mahiga nyuguto, nĩ ũndũ nĩmetĩkĩtie atĩ Johana aarĩ mũnabii.”
English: But if we say, ‘From men,’ all the people will stone us, for they are persuaded that John was a prophet.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩcookeria Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtiũĩ woimĩte kũ.”
English: They answered that they didn’t know where it was from.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “O na niĩ ndikũmwĩra njĩkaga maũndũ maya na ũhoti ũrĩkũ.”
English: Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩhe andũ ũhoro na ngerekano akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũmwe nĩahaandire mũgũnda wakemĩthabibũ, naakĩũkomborithiaarĩmiangĩ, agĩcookaagĩthiĩkũndũkũrayanaagĩikarakuoihinda iraaya.
English: He began to tell the people this parable: “A ‡ man planted a vineyard and rented it out to some farmers, and went into another country for a long time.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ya magetha yakinya-rĩ, agĩtũma ndungata yake kũrĩ akombori acio nĩgeetha mamĩnengere maciaro mamwe ma mũgũnda ũcio wa mĩthabibũ. No akombori acio makĩmĩhũũra na makĩmĩingata ĩtarĩ na kĩndũ.
English: At the proper season, he sent a servant to the farmers to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the farmers beat him and sent him away empty.

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩtũma ndungata ĩngĩ, ĩyo o nayo makĩmĩhũũra makĩmĩconorithia na makĩmĩingata ĩtarĩ na kĩndũ.
English: He sent yet another servant, and they also beat him and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.

Kikuyu: O rĩngĩ agĩtũma ndungata ya gatatũ, nao makĩmĩtiihangia na makĩmĩingata.
English: He sent yet a third, and they also wounded him and threw him out.

Kikuyu: “Nake mwene mũgũnda ũcio wa mĩthabibũ akĩĩyũria atĩrĩ, ‘Ngwĩka atĩa? Ngũtũma mũrũ wakwa, ũrĩa nyendete; hihi mahota kũmũhe gĩtĩĩo’.
English: The lord of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son. It may be that seeing him, they will respect him.’

Kikuyu: “No rĩrĩa akombori acio maamuonire, makĩarĩrĩria ũhoro ũcio. Makĩĩrana atĩrĩ, ‘Ũyũ nĩwe ũkaagaya mũgũnda ũyũ. Nĩtũmũũragei nĩguo igai rĩake rĩtuĩke riitũ.’
English: “But when the farmers saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.’

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩmũruta nja ya mũgũnda ũcio wa mĩthabibũ, makĩmũũraga. “Rĩu-rĩ, mwene mũgũnda ũcio wa mĩthabibũ-rĩ, akaameka atĩa?
English: Then they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do to them?

Kikuyu: Agooka oorage akombori acio, acooke ahe andũ angĩ mũgũnda ũcio.” Rĩrĩa andũ maiguire ũguo, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Kũroaga gũtuĩka ũguo!”
English: He will come and destroy these farmers, and will give the vineyard to others.” When they heard that, they said, “May that never be!”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmarora, akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Maandĩko marĩa mandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “ ‘Ihiga rĩrĩa aaki maaregire, nĩrĩo rĩtuĩkĩte ihiga inene rĩa koine’, ũguo nĩ kuuga atĩa?
English: But he looked at them and said, “Then what is this that is written, ‘The stone which the builders rejected was made the chief cornerstone’?*

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũkaagwĩra ihiga rĩu, nĩrĩkamuunanga, no ũrĩa rĩkaagwĩra nĩrĩkamũhehenja.”
English: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, but it will crush whomever it falls on to dust.”

Kikuyu: Arutani a watho na athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene magĩcaria ũrĩa mangĩmũnyiitithia o na ihenya tondũ nĩmamenyire aaheanire ngerekano ĩyo akĩaria ũhoro wao. No nĩmetigagĩra andũ.
English: The chief priests and the scribes sought to lay hands on him that very hour, but they feared the people—for they knew he had spoken this parable against them.

Kikuyu: O makĩmũtuĩragia-rĩ, makĩmũtũmĩra andũ a kũmũthigaana metuĩte andũ ega. Nao magĩthiĩ nĩgeetha mamũnyiite akiuga ũndũ mũũru, nĩguo mamũneane moko-inĩ ma wathani wa barũthi.
English: They watched him and sent out spies, who pretended to be righteous, that they might trap him in something he said, so as to deliver him up to the power and authority of the governor.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio athigaani acio makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, nĩtũũĩ wee waragia na ũkarutana maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire, na atĩ ndũtĩagĩra andũ maũthĩ, no wee ũrutanaga ũhoro wa Ngai na ma, ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire.
English: They asked him, “Teacher, we know that you say and teach what is right, and aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God.

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩtwagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũrutĩra Kaisari igooti kana ca?”*
English: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”

Kikuyu: Nowe Jesũ akĩona ũhinga ũrĩa maarĩ naguo akĩmeera atĩrĩ,
English: But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them, “Why do you test me?

Kikuyu: “Ta nyoniai mbeeca cianyu. Mbica ĩno na rĩĩtwa rĩĩrĩ nĩ cia ũ?”
English: Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?” They answered, “Caesar’s.”

Kikuyu: Makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ cia Kaisari.” Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Kĩrutagĩrei Kaisari kĩrĩa kĩrĩ gĩake, na mũrutagĩre Ngai kĩrĩa kĩrĩ gĩake.”
English: He said to them, “Then give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩaga ũndũ wa kũmũnyiita naguo harĩ maũndũ marĩa aaririe arĩ mbere ya andũ. Na tondũ wa ũrĩa maagegirio nĩ ũrĩa aamacookeirie ũhoro-rĩ, magĩkira ki.
English: They weren’t able to trap him in his words before the people. They marveled at his answer and were silent.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, andũ amwe a Asadukai, arĩa moigaga atĩ gũtirĩ ũhoro wa kũriũka, magĩũka kũrĩ Jesũ, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ,
English: Some of the Sadducees came to him, those who deny that there is a resurrection.

Kikuyu: “Mũrutani, Musa aatwandĩkĩire atĩ mũrũ wa nyina na mũndũ angĩkua atige mũtumia atarĩ na ciana-rĩ, mũndũ ũcio no nginya ahikie mũtumia ũcio watigwo nĩguo aciarĩre mũrũ wa nyina ciana.
English: They asked him, “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man’s brother dies having a wife, and he is childless, his brother should take the wife and raise up children for his brother.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, kwarĩ aanake mũgwanja a nyina ũmwe. Wa mbere akĩhikania na agĩkua, agĩtiga mũtumia atarĩ na ciana.
English: There were therefore seven brothers. The first took a wife, and died childless.

Kikuyu: Nake wa keerĩ,
English: The second took her as wife, and he died childless.

Kikuyu: na wa gatatũ makĩmũhikia, na makĩrũmanĩrĩra ũguo nginya wa mũgwanja; othe magĩkua, makĩmũtiga atarĩ na ciana.
English: The third took her, and likewise the seven all left no children, and died.

Kikuyu: Marigĩrĩrio-inĩ, mũtumia ũcio o nake agĩkua.
English: Afterward the woman also died.

Kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, hĩndĩ ya kũriũka agaakorwo arĩ mũtumia wa ũ harĩ acio mũgwanja, nĩgũkorwo othe nĩmamũhikĩtie?”
English: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them will she be? For the seven had her as a wife.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Andũ a ihinda rĩĩrĩ nĩo mahikaga na makahikania.
English: Jesus said to them, “The children of this age marry and are given in marriage.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, arĩa magaatuuo aagĩrĩru a gũkinya ihinda rĩu o na gũkinyanĩrio ihinda rĩa kũriũka kwa arĩa akuũ-rĩ, acio matikahikanagia o na kana mahikanagie.
English: But those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio matigacooka gũkua; nĩgũkorwo makahaana ta araika. Andũ acio magakorwo marĩ ciana cia Ngai, nĩgũkorwo nĩ ciana cia ũhoro ũcio wa kũriũka.
English: For they can’t die any more, for they are like the angels and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.

Kikuyu: Naguo ũhoro wa atĩ andũ arĩa akuũ nĩmariũkagio-rĩ, Musa o nake nĩawonanirie harĩa haarĩ kĩhinga kĩrĩa gĩakanaga mwaki na gĩtihĩe, rĩrĩa eetire Mwathani ‘Ngai wa Iburahĩmu, na Ngai wa Isaaka, na Ngai wa Jakubu.’
English: But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed at the bush, when he called the Lord ‘The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’ *

Kikuyu: We ti Ngai wa arĩa akuũ, no nĩ wa arĩa marĩ muoyo, nĩgũkorwo harĩ we andũ othe arĩa marĩ muoyo matũũragio nĩwe.”
English: Now he is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for all are alive to him.”

Kikuyu: Nao andũ amwe a arutani a watho makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, nĩwoiga wega!”
English: Some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you speak well.”

Kikuyu: Na gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wageririe kũmũũria ciũria ingĩ.
English: They didn’t dare to ask him any more questions.

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcooka akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Andũ moigaga Kristũ nĩ mũriũ wa Daudi nĩ ũndũ wa kĩĩ?
English: He said to them, “Why do they say that the Christ is David’s son?

Kikuyu: Daudi we mwene nĩoigĩte ibuku-inĩ rĩa Thaburi atĩrĩ: “ ‘Mwathani nĩerire Mwathani wakwa atĩrĩ: “Ikara guoko-inĩ gwakwa kwa ũrĩo,
English: David himself says in the book of Psalms, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,

Kikuyu: nginya ngaatũma thũ ciaku ituĩke gaturwa ka makinya maku.” ’
English: until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.” ’ *

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, Daudi aamwĩtire ‘Mwathani’. Angĩgĩcooka gũtuĩka mũriũ wake atĩa?”
English: “David therefore calls him Lord, so how is he his son?”

Kikuyu: Oandũacioothemathikĩrĩirie, Jesũakĩĩraarutwoakeatĩrĩ,
English: In the hearing of all the people, he said to his disciples,

Kikuyu: “Mwĩmenyerereiarutaniawatho. O mendaga gũthiĩ mehumbĩte nguo ndaaya, na makenda kũgeithagio ndũnyũ-inĩ, na gũikaragĩra itĩ iria cia bata thunagogi-inĩ, o na gũikara handũ ha andũ arĩa atĩĩku maruga-inĩ.
English: “Beware of those scribes who like to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the marketplaces, the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts;

Kikuyu: No nĩo matoonyagĩrĩra atumia a ndigwa makamatunya nyũmba ciao, na nĩguo meyonanie, makahooya mahooya maraihu. Andũ ta acio nĩmakaherithio mũno makĩria.”
English: who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, aatiira maitho, Jesũ akĩona itonga igĩĩkĩra mĩhothi yao harĩa heekagĩrwo kĩgĩĩna kĩa hekarũ.
English: He looked up and saw the rich people who were putting their gifts into the treasury.

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩona mũtumia mũthĩĩni, warĩ wa ndigwa, agĩĩkĩra tũthendi twĩrĩ o ho thĩinĩ.
English: He saw a certain poor widow casting in two small brass coins.†

Kikuyu: Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, mũtumia ũyũ mũthĩĩni wa ndigwa nĩahotha mbeeca nyingĩ gũkĩra andũ arĩa angĩ othe.
English: He said, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow put in more than all of them,

Kikuyu: Andũ acio angĩ othe maaheana kũringana na ũtonga wao; no mũtumia ũyũ o na arĩ mũthĩĩni, ahotha kĩrĩa gĩothe oima nakĩo gĩa kũmũtũũria.”
English: for all these put in gifts for God from their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, put in all that she had to live on.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, arutwo amwe ake nĩmaragia ũhoro wa hekarũ, ũrĩa yagemetio na mahiga mathaka na iheo iria ciarutĩirwo Ngai. No Jesũ akiuga atĩrĩ,
English: As some were talking about the temple and how it was decorated with beautiful stones and gifts, he said,

Kikuyu: “Ha ũhoro wa mĩako ĩno mũrona-rĩ, hĩndĩ nĩĩroka rĩrĩa gũtagakorwo ihiga rĩrĩ igũrũ wa rĩrĩa rĩngĩ; mothe nĩmakamomorwo.”
English: “As for these things which you see, the days will come in which there will not be left here one stone on another that will not be thrown down.”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, nĩ rĩ maũndũ macio magekĩka? Na kĩmenyithia kĩa atĩ marĩ hakuhĩ gwĩkĩka nĩ kĩrĩkũ?”
English: They asked him, “Teacher, so when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are about to happen?”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Mwĩmenyagĩrĩrei mũtikanaheenio. Nĩgũkorwo andũ aingĩ nĩmagooka na rĩĩtwa rĩakwa makiugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ niĩ We,’ na ningĩ mamwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Ihinda nĩrĩkuhĩrĩirie.’ Mũtikanamarũmĩrĩre.
