English: He said, “Watch out that you don’t get led astray, for many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he‡,’ and, ‘The time is at hand.’ Therefore don’t follow them.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũkaigua ũhoro wa mbaara na wa ngũĩ, mũtikanetigĩre. Maũndũ macio no nginya mekĩke mbere, no ũguo ti kuuga ithirĩro nĩikinyu.”
English: When you hear of wars and disturbances, don’t be terrified, for these things must happen first, but the end won’t come immediately.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Rũrĩrĩ nĩrũgookĩrĩra rũrĩa rũngĩ, na ũthamaki ũũkĩrĩre ũrĩa ũngĩ.
English: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkaagĩa na ithingithia nene mũno, na ngʼaragu, na mĩrimũ mĩũru kũndũ na kũndũ, na maũndũ ma kũmakania na imenyithia nene kuuma igũrũ.
English: There will be great earthquakes, famines, and plagues in various places. There will be terrors and great signs from heaven.

Kikuyu: “No maũndũ macio mothe matanakinya-rĩ, nĩmũkanyiitwo, na mũnyariirwo na mũneanwo thunagogi-inĩ na njeera o na mũtwarwo mbere ya athamaki na aathani. Maũndũ macio mothe mũgeekwo nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa.
English: But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and will persecute you, delivering you up to synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors for my name’s sake.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ ũgaakorwo ũrĩ mweke wanyu wa kũmarutĩra ũira.
English: It will turn out as a testimony for you.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũtikanatangĩke mũgĩĩciiria o mbere, ũrĩa mũgeeciirĩrĩra.
English: Settle it therefore in your hearts not to meditate beforehand how to answer,

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩngamũhe ciugo na ũũgĩ iria thũ cianyu itakahota gwĩtiiria kana gũkararia.
English: for I will give you a mouth and wisdom which all your adversaries will not be able to withstand or to contradict.

Kikuyu: Nĩmũgakunyanĩrwo o na nĩ aciari anyu, na arĩa mũciaranĩirwo nao, na andũ a mbarĩ cianyu, o na arata, na nĩmakooragithia amwe anyu.
English: You will be handed over even by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. They will cause some of you to be put to death.

Kikuyu: Andũ othe nĩmakamũmena nĩ ũndũ wakwa.
English: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, gũtirĩ rũcuĩrĩ o na rũmwe rwa mũtwe wanyu rũkoora.
English: And not a hair of your head will perish.

Kikuyu: Kũũmĩrĩria kwanyu nĩkuo gũgaatũma mũhonokie mĩoyo yanyu.
English: “By your endurance you will win your lives.

Kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa mũkoona Jerusalemu kũrigiicĩirio nĩ thigari cia mbaara, hĩndĩ ĩyo mũkaamenya atĩ kũniinwo kwarĩo kũrĩ hakuhĩ.
English: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation is at hand.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo arĩa marĩ Judea nĩmorĩre irĩma-inĩ, na arĩa marĩ thĩinĩ wa itũũra rĩu inene nĩmoime kuo, nao arĩa marĩ ngʼongo-inĩ matikarĩtoonye.
English: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains. Let those who are in the middle of her depart. Let those who are in the country not enter therein.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ihinda rĩu nĩrĩo rĩgaakorwo rĩrĩ rĩa kũherithanio, nĩguo maũndũ marĩa mothe mandĩkĩtwo mahingio.
English: For these are days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ atumia arĩa magaakorwo marĩ na nda, na arĩa magaakorwo makĩongithia twana matukũ macio nĩmagathĩĩnĩka-ĩ! Nĩgũkorwo bũrũri ũyũ nĩũkagĩa na kĩnyariirĩko kĩnene na mangʼũrĩ ma gũũkĩrĩra andũ aya.
English: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who nurse infants in those days! For there will be great distress in the land and wrath to this people.

Kikuyu: Nĩmakooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, o na matahwo matwarwo mabũrũri mothe ma thĩ. Itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu nĩrĩkarangĩrĩrio nĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ o nginya mahinda ma andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ mahingio.
English: They will fall by the edge of the sword, and will be led captive into all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.

Kikuyu: “Na nĩgũkagĩa imenyithia thĩinĩ wa riũa na mweri-inĩ na njata-inĩ. Nakuo gũkũ thĩ, ndũrĩrĩ nĩikaigua ruo na igegio nĩ mũrurumo wa iria na makũmbĩ ma maaĩ makĩhũũrana.
English: “There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars; and on the earth anxiety of nations, in perplexity for the roaring of the sea and the waves;

Kikuyu: Andũ nĩmakaringĩka nĩ ũndũ wa kũmaka, na guoya wa maũndũ marĩa magooka thĩ, nĩgũkorwo maahinya ma igũrũ nĩmakenyenyio.
English: men fainting for fear and for expectation of the things which are coming on the world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakona Mũrũ wa Mũndũ agĩũka arĩ thĩinĩ wa itu arĩ na hinya na riiri mũingĩ.
English: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maũndũ macio makaambĩrĩria gwĩkĩka-rĩ, mũkaarũgama mũtiire mĩtwe yanyu na igũrũ, nĩgũkorwo hĩndĩ yanyu ya gũkũũrwo nĩĩkuhĩrĩirie.”
English: But when these things begin to happen, look up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is near.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩmahe ngerekano ĩno: “Ta rorai mũtĩ wa mũkũyũ na mĩtĩ ĩyo ĩngĩ yothe.
English: He told them a parable. “See the fig tree and all the trees.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa yathundũra mathangũ, mũmĩonaga na mũkamenya atĩ ihinda rĩa riũa rĩrĩ hakuhĩ.
English: When they are already budding, you see it and know by your own selves that the summer is already near.

Kikuyu: Ũguo noguo hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũkoona maũndũ macio mothe magĩkĩka, mũkaamenya o wega atĩ ũthamaki wa Ngai nĩũkuhĩrĩirie.
English: Even so you also, when you see these things happening, know that God’s Kingdom is near.

Kikuyu: “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, rũciaro rũrũ rũtigathira maũndũ macio mothe matahingĩtio.
English: Most certainly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things are accomplished.

Kikuyu: Igũrũ na thĩ nĩigathira, no ciugo ciakwa itigathira.
English: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will by no means pass away.

Kikuyu: “Mwĩmenyererei, ngoro cianyu itikanakorwo itahĩtwo nĩ ndĩa, na ũrĩĩu na metanga ma muoyo ũyũ, naguo mũthenya ũcio ũmũkorerere ta mũtego o rĩrĩa mũtangĩĩgereria.
English: “So be careful, or your hearts will be loaded down with carousing, drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that day will come on you suddenly.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũndũ ũyũ ũgaakorerera andũ arĩa othe matũũraga thĩ-ĩno.
English: For it will come like a snare on all those who dwell on the surface of all the earth.

Kikuyu: Ikaragai mwĩiguĩte hĩndĩ ciothe, na mũhooyage nĩguo mũkaahota kũũrĩra maũndũ marĩa makiriĩ gwĩkĩka, na nĩguo mũkaahota kũrũgama mbere ya Mũrũ wa Mũndũ.”
English: Therefore be watchful all the time, praying that you may be counted worthy to escape all these things that will happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.”

Kikuyu: Na o mũthenya Jesũ nĩarutanaga hekarũ-inĩ, na hwaĩ-inĩ agathiĩ gũkoma kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩrĩa gĩĩtagwo Kĩrĩma kĩa Mĩtamaiyũ.
English: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, and every night he would go out and spend the night on the mountain that is called Olivet.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ othe nĩmathiiaga hekarũ-inĩ rũciinĩ tene kũmũthikĩrĩria.
English: All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Gĩathĩ kĩa Mĩgate ĩrĩa Ĩtekĩrĩtwo Ndawa ya Kũimbia, kĩrĩa gĩĩtagwo Bathaka,* nĩgĩakuhĩrĩirie,
English: Now the feast of unleavened bread, which is called the Passover, was approaching.

Kikuyu: nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na arutani a watho nĩmacaragia ũrĩa mangĩũragithia Jesũ, nĩgũkorwo nĩmetigagĩra mũingĩ wa andũ.
English: The chief priests and the scribes sought how they might put him to death, for they feared the people.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Shaitani agĩtoonya thĩinĩ wa Judasi ũrĩa Mũisikariota, ũmwe wa arutwo arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ.
English: Satan entered into Judas, who was also called Iscariot, who was counted with the twelve.

Kikuyu: Nake Judasi agĩthiĩ kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na kũrĩ anene a arangĩri a hekarũ makarĩkanĩre ũrĩa angĩkunyanĩra Jesũ.
English: He went away and talked with the chief priests and captains about how he might deliver him to them.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩkena na magĩtĩkĩra kũmũhe mbeeca.
English: They were glad, and agreed to give him money.

Kikuyu: Nake agĩĩtĩkĩra, na akĩambĩrĩria gũcaria mweke wa kũneana Jesũ kũrĩ o rĩrĩa hatarĩ na andũ.
English: He consented and sought an opportunity to deliver him to them in the absence of the multitude.

Kikuyu: Naguo mũthenya wa kũrĩa Mĩgate ĩrĩa Ĩtekĩrĩtwo Ndawa ya Kũimbia ũgĩkinya, rĩrĩa gatũrũme ka Bathaka kaarutagwo igongona.
English: The day of unleavened bread came, on which the Passover must be sacrificed.

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩtũma Petero na Johana, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi mũ- gatũhaarĩrie irio cia Bathaka, tũrĩe.”
English: Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover for us, that we may eat.”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũkwenda tũgacihaarĩrie kũ?”
English: They said to him, “Where do you want us to prepare?”

Kikuyu: Akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũgĩtoonya itũũra-inĩ inene-rĩ, nĩmũgũcemania na mũndũ ũkuuĩte ndigithũ ĩrĩ na maaĩ. Mũmũrũmĩrĩre o nginya nyũmba ĩrĩa egũtoonya.
English: He said to them, “Behold, when you have entered into the city, a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him into the house which he enters.

Kikuyu: Mwatoonya mwĩre mũndũ ũrĩa mwene nyũmba ĩyo atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrutani aroria atĩrĩ: Nyũmba ya ageni ĩrĩ ha, harĩa ingĩrĩĩra Bathaka na arutwo akwa?’
English: Tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says to you, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?” ’

Kikuyu: Nake nĩekũmuonia nyũmba nene ya igũrũ ĩrĩ na indo kuo. Haarĩriai irio cia Bathaka kuo.”
English: He will show you a large, furnished upper room. Make preparations there.”

Kikuyu: Nao magĩthiĩ, magĩkora maũndũ matariĩ o ta ũrĩa Jesũ aamerĩte. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩhaarĩria irio cia Bathaka.
English: They went, found things as Jesus had told them, and they prepared the Passover.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ihinda rĩakinyire, Jesũ na atũmwo ake magĩikara metha-inĩ.
English: When the hour had come, he sat down with the twelve apostles.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩngoretwo ndĩĩrirĩirie mũno kũrĩanĩra Bathaka ĩno na inyuĩ itananyariirwo.
English: He said to them, “I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer,

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ndikamĩrĩa rĩngĩ nginya rĩrĩa ĩgaakinyanĩrio ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai.”
English: for I tell you, I will no longer by any means eat of it until it is fulfilled in God’s Kingdom.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcooka akĩoya gĩkombe, agĩcookeria Ngai ngaatho, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Oyai, mũnyuanĩre.
English: He received a cup, and when he had given thanks, he said, “Take this and share it among yourselves,

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ngũmwĩra atĩ kuuma rĩu ndikanyua rĩngĩ ndibei ya maciaro ma mĩthabibũ, o nginya rĩrĩa ũthamaki wa Ngai ũgooka.”
English: for I tell you, I will not drink at all again from the fruit of the vine, until God’s Kingdom comes.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩoya mũgate, na aarĩkia gũcookeria Ngai ngaatho, akĩwenyũranga akĩmahe akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩguo mwĩrĩ wakwa ũrĩa ũheanĩtwo nĩ ũndũ wanyu, ĩkagai ũũ nĩguo mũndirikanage.”
English: He took bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke and gave it to them, saying, “This is my body which is given for you. Do this in memory of me.”

Kikuyu: Maarĩkia kũrĩa agĩĩka o ũguo, akĩoya gĩkombe, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Gĩkombe gĩkĩ nĩkĩo kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa kĩerũ thĩinĩ wa thakame yakwa ĩrĩa ĩgũitwo nĩ ũndũ wanyu.
English: Likewise, he took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, guoko kwa ũrĩa ũrĩngunyanĩra kũrĩ hamwe na niĩ metha-inĩ ĩno.
English: But behold, the hand of him who betrays me is with me on the table.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩegwĩkwo o ta ũrĩa gũtuĩtwo, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũkũmũkunyanĩra kaĩ arĩ na haaro-ĩ!”
English: The Son of Man indeed goes as it has been determined, but woe to that man through whom he is betrayed!”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩambĩrĩria kũũrania gatagatĩ-inĩ kao nĩ ũrĩkũ wao ũngĩka ũguo.
English: They began to question among themselves which of them it was who would do this thing.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩmagĩire na ngarari makĩũrania nũũ gatagatĩ-inĩ kao ũngĩtuuo mũnene.
English: A dispute also arose among them, which of them was considered to be greatest.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Athamaki a andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ nĩmenenehagia igũrũ rĩa andũ ao; nao acio maathanaga nĩo meĩĩtaga Ateithania.
English: He said to them, “The kings of the nations lord it over them, and those who have authority over them are called ‘benefactors.’

Kikuyu: No inyuĩ mũtiagĩrĩirwo nĩkũhaana ũguo. Handũ ha ũguo, ũrĩa mũnene gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu agĩrĩirwo kũhaana ta ũrĩa mũnini mũno, nake ũrĩa wathanaga atuĩke ta ũrĩa ũtungataga.
English: But not so with you. Rather, the one who is greater among you, let him become as the younger, and one who is governing, as one who serves.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, nũũ mũnene, nĩ ũrĩa ũikaraga metha-inĩ kũrĩa kana nĩ ũrĩa ũramũtungata? Githĩ ti ũrĩa ũikaraga metha-inĩ? No niĩ ndũire na inyuĩ, haana ta ũrĩa ũmũtungatagĩra.
English: For who is greater, one who sits at the table, or one who serves? Isn’t it he who sits at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩ inyuĩ mũkoretwo mũrĩ hamwe na niĩ hĩndĩ ya magerio makwa.
English: “But you are those who have continued with me in my trials.

Kikuyu: Na o ta ũrĩa Baba aheete ũthamaki, o na niĩ nĩndamũhe ũthamaki,
English: I confer on you a kingdom, even as my Father conferred on me,

Kikuyu: nĩguo mũkarĩĩagĩra na mũkanyuuagĩra metha-inĩ yakwa ũthamaki-inĩ wakwa, na ningĩ mũikarĩire itĩ-cia-ũnene, mũtuagĩre mĩhĩrĩga ĩrĩa ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ ya Isiraeli ciira.
English: that you may eat and drink at my table in my Kingdom. You will sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

Kikuyu: “Simoni, Simoni, atĩrĩ, Shaitani nĩahooete rũtha nĩguo agũthũngũthie o ta ũrĩa ngano ĩthũngũthagio.
English: The Lord said, “Simon, Simon, behold, Satan asked to have all of you, that he might sift you as wheat,

Kikuyu: No wee, Simoni nĩngũhooeire nĩgeetha wĩtĩkio waku ndũgathire. Na warĩkia kũnjookerera, ũũmagĩrĩrie ariũ na aarĩ a thoguo.”
English: but I prayed for you, that your faith wouldn’t fail. You, when once you have turned again, establish your brothers.”†

Kikuyu: Nowe agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, nĩndĩhaarĩirie gũikio njeera o na gũkuanĩra hamwe nawe.”
English: He said to him, “Lord, I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death!”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Petero, ngũkwĩra atĩrĩ, ũmũthĩ ngũkũ ĩtanakũga, nĩũrĩngaana maita matatũ uuge atĩ ndũnjũũĩ.”
English: He said, “I tell you, Peter, the rooster will by no means crow today until you deny that you know me three times.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcooka akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Rĩrĩa ndamũtũmire mũtarĩ na kabeti, kana mũhuko, o na kana iraatũ-rĩ, nĩ harĩ kĩndũ mwagire?” Nao magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ kĩndũ twagire.”
English: He said to them, “When I sent you out without purse, bag, and sandals, did you lack anything?” They said, “Nothing.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Rĩu akorwo ũrĩ na kabeti kana mũhuko-rĩ, kuua; na akorwo ndũrĩ na rũhiũ rwa njora-rĩ, endia nguo yaku ya igũrũ, ũrũgũre.
English: Then he said to them, “But now, whoever has a purse, let him take it, and likewise a bag. Whoever has none, let him sell his cloak, and buy a sword.

Kikuyu: Na ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũhoro ũrĩa wandĩkĩtwo atĩ, ‘Nĩataranĩirio hamwe na arĩa aagarari watho’ no nginya ũhinge harĩ niĩ. Nĩgũkorwo maũndũ marĩa maandĩkĩtwo igũrũ rĩakwa nĩ hĩndĩ marahinga.”
English: For I tell you that this which is written must still be fulfilled in me: ‘He was counted with transgressors.’* For that which concerns me is being fulfilled.”

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, ta rora haha tũrĩ na hiũ cia njora igĩrĩ.” Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Icio nĩ njiganu.”
English: They said, “Lord, behold, here are two swords.” He said to them, “That is enough.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akiumagara agĩthiĩ Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Mĩtamaiyũ o ta ũrĩa amenyerete gwĩkaga, nao arutwo ake makĩmũrũmĩrĩra.
English: He came out and went, as his custom was, to the Mount of Olives. His disciples also followed him.

Kikuyu: Na aakinya ho akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Hooyai mũtikagwe magerio-inĩ.”
English: When he was at the place, he said to them, “Pray that you don’t enter into temptation.”

Kikuyu: Akĩehera ho, agĩthĩi ta itĩĩna rĩa harĩa mũndũ angĩhota gũikia ihiga, agĩturia ndu akĩhooya,
English: He was withdrawn from them about a stone’s throw, and he knelt down and prayed,

Kikuyu: akiuga atĩrĩ, “Baba, ũngĩenda-rĩ, njehereria gĩkombe gĩkĩ; no ti ũrĩa niĩ ngwenda, no nĩ ũrĩa wee ũkwenda.”
English: saying, “Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done.”

Kikuyu: Na mũraika akĩmuumĩrĩra oimĩte igũrũ, akĩmuongerera hinya.
English: An angel from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him.

Kikuyu: Nake agĩkĩrĩrĩria kũhooya, arĩ na ruo rũnene ngoro-inĩ, nayo thithino yake yahaanaga ta matata manene ma thakame magĩtaata thĩ.
English: Being in agony, he prayed more earnestly. His sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.

Kikuyu: Aatiga kũhooya agĩcooka harĩ arutwo, akĩmakora makomete, moorĩtwo nĩ hinya nĩ kĩeha.
English: When he rose up from his prayer, he came to the disciples and found them sleeping because of grief,

Kikuyu: Akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũkomete nĩkĩ? Ũkĩrai mũhooe nĩgeetha mũtikagwe magerio-inĩ.”
English: and said to them, “Why do you sleep? Rise and pray that you may not enter into temptation.”

Kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo aragia, hagĩũka gĩkundi kĩa andũ, nake mũndũ ũrĩa wetagwo Judasi, ũmwe wa arutwo arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ, nĩwe wamatongoretie. Agĩkuhĩrĩria Jesũ amũmumunye,
English: While he was still speaking, a crowd appeared. He who was called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He came near to Jesus to kiss him.

Kikuyu: nowe Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Judasi, ũgũkunyanĩra Mũrũ wa Mũndũ na kũmũmumunya?”
English: But Jesus said to him, “Judas, do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa arũmĩrĩri a Jesũ monire ũrĩa kwerekeire gwĩkĩka, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, tũmahũũre na hiũ ciitũ cia njora?”
English: When those who were around him saw what was about to happen, they said to him, “Lord, shall we strike with the sword?”

Kikuyu: Nake ũmwe wao agĩtinia gũtũ kwa ũrĩo kwa ndungata ya mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene.
English: A certain one of them struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.

Kikuyu: Nowe Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũhoro ũcio nĩũkinye hau!” Agĩcooka akĩhutia gũtũ kwa mũndũ ũcio, akĩmũhonia.
English: But Jesus answered, “Let me at least do this”—and he touched his ear and healed him.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jesũ akĩũria athĩnjĩrĩ-Ngai arĩa anene, na anene a arangĩri a hekarũ, o na athuuri, arĩa mookĩte kũmũnyiita atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ ndĩ mũtongoria wa akararia a watho, atĩ nĩkĩo muoka kũũnyiita mũrĩ na hiũ cia njora na njũgũma?
English: Jesus said to the chief priests, captains of the temple, and elders, who had come against him, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs?

Kikuyu: Ndĩratũũraga na inyuĩ hekarũ-inĩ o mũthenya, na mũtirĩ mwageria kũnyiita. No rĩrĩ, rĩĩrĩ nĩrĩo ithaa rĩanyu, ithaa rĩrĩa wathani wa nduma ũraathana.”
English: When I was with you in the temple daily, you didn’t stretch out your hands against me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo makĩmũnyiita, makĩmũruta hau makĩmũtwara kwa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, nake Petero akĩmũrũmĩrĩra arĩ o haraaya.
English: They seized him and led him away, and brought him into the high priest’s house. But Peter followed from a distance.

Kikuyu: Nao maarĩkia gwakia mwaki o nja gatagatĩ na gũikara thĩ hamwe, Petero nake agĩikara thĩ hamwe nao.
English: When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and had sat down together, Peter sat among them.

Kikuyu: Ndungata ĩmwe ya mũirĩtu ĩkĩmuona na ũtheri wa mwaki aikarĩte hau, ĩkĩmũrora wega, ĩkiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ oima hamwe nake.”
English: A certain servant girl saw him as he sat in the light, and looking intently at him, said, “This man also was with him.”

Kikuyu: Nowe agĩkaana ũhoro ũcio, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũirĩtu ũyũ, niĩ ndimũũĩ.”
English: He denied Jesus, saying, “Woman, I don’t know him.”

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa kahinda kanini mũndũ ũngĩ akĩmuona, akiuga atĩrĩ, “O nawe ũrĩ ũmwe wao.” Petero akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ, niĩ ndirĩ wao!”
English: After a little while someone else saw him and said, “You also are one of them!” But Peter answered, “Man, I am not!”

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa ithaa rĩmwe, mũndũ ũngĩ akiuga agwatĩirie atĩrĩ, “Hatirĩ nganja mũndũ ũyũ moima hamwe nake, nĩgũkorwo o nake nĩ Mũgalili.”
English: After about one hour passed, another confidently affirmed, saying, “Truly this man also was with him, for he is a Galilean!”

Kikuyu: Petero agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ, niĩ ndiũĩ ũrĩa ũroiga!” Na hĩndĩ o ĩyo oigaga ũguo, ngũkũ ĩgĩkũga.
English: But Peter said, “Man, I don’t know what you are talking about!” Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.

Kikuyu: Mwathani akĩĩhũgũra akĩrora Petero. Nake Petero akĩririkana ũrĩa Mwathani aamwĩrĩte atĩ, “Ũmũthĩ ngũkũ ĩtanakũga nĩũrĩngaana maita matatũ.”
English: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the Lord’s word, how he said to him, “Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.”

Kikuyu: Nake agĩthiĩ nja, akĩrĩra arĩ na ruo rũingĩ.
English: He went out, and wept bitterly.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maarangĩrĩte Jesũ makĩambĩrĩria kũmũnyũrũria makĩmũhũũraga.
English: The men who held Jesus mocked him and beat him.

Kikuyu: Makĩmuoha maitho na gĩtambaya makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Toiga nũũ wakũgũtha.”
English: Having blindfolded him, they struck him on the face and asked him, “Prophesy! Who is the one who struck you?”

Kikuyu: Na nĩmamwĩrire maũndũ mangĩ maingĩ ma kũmũruma.
English: They spoke many other things against him, insulting him.

Kikuyu: Rũciinĩ gwakĩa, kĩama gĩa athuuri arĩa atongoria a andũ, hamwe na athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na arutani a watho, gĩgĩcemania, nake Jesũ akĩrehwo mbere yao.
English: As soon as it was day, the assembly of the elders of the people were gathered together, both chief priests and scribes, and they led him away into their council, saying,

Kikuyu: Makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Akorwo nĩwe Kristũ, twĩre.” Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ingĩmwĩra, mũtingĩnjĩtĩkia,
English: “If you are the Christ, tell us.” But he said to them, “If I tell you, you won’t believe,

Kikuyu: na ingĩmũũria kĩũria, mũtingĩnjoke- ria.
English: and if I ask, you will in no way answer me or let me go.

Kikuyu: No kuuma rĩu, Mũrũ wa Mũndũ arĩkoragwo aikarĩte guoko-inĩ kwa ũrĩo kwa Ngai ũrĩa Mwene- Hinya.”
English: From now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”

Kikuyu: Othe makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wee ũkĩrĩ Mũrũ wa Ngai?” Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩmwamenya nĩ kuuga atĩ nĩ niĩ we.”
English: They all said, “Are you then the Son of God?” He said to them, “You say it, because I am.”

Kikuyu: Makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Tũgũkĩenda ũira ũngĩ wakĩ? Nĩtweiguĩra ũhoro kuuma kanua gake mwene.”
English: They said, “Why do we need any more witness? For we ourselves have heard from his own mouth!”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo kĩũngano kĩu gĩothe gĩgĩũkĩra, gĩkĩmũtwara kũrĩ Pilato.
English: The whole company of them rose up and brought him before Pilate.

Kikuyu: Nakĩo gĩkĩambĩrĩria kũmũthitanga gĩkiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩtuonete mũndũ ũyũ akĩhĩtithia rũrĩrĩ rwitũ. Nĩakararagia ũhoro wa kũrutĩra Kaisari igooti na agetuaga atĩ nĩwe Kristũ, na nĩ mũthamaki.”
English: They began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man perverting the nation, forbidding paying taxes to Caesar, and saying that he himself is Christ, a king.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Pilato akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe mũthamaki wa Ayahudi?” Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ, ũguo woiga nĩguo.”
English: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered him, “So you say.”

Kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩĩra athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na andũ acio angĩ atĩrĩ, “Ndikuona ũndũ ingĩthitangĩra mũndũ ũyũ.”
English: Pilate said to the chief priests and the multitudes, “I find no basis for a charge against this man.”

Kikuyu: Nao magĩthiĩ na mbere kuuga magwatĩirie atĩrĩ, “Nĩatũmĩte andũ mookanĩrĩre Judea guothe nĩ ũndũ wa ũrutani wake. Aambĩrĩirie Galili na nĩokĩte nginya o gũkũ.”
English: But they insisted, saying, “He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee even to this place.”

Kikuyu: Pilato aigua ũguo, akĩũria kana mũndũ ũcio nĩ wa kuuma Galili.
English: But when Pilate heard Galilee mentioned, he asked if the man was a Galilean.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aamenyire atĩ Jesũ aarĩ wa bũrũri ũrĩa wathanagwo nĩ Herode, akiuga atwarwo kũrĩ Herode, ũrĩa wakoretwo arĩ Jerusalemu ihinda-inĩ rĩu.
English: When he found out that he was in Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem during those days.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Herode oonire Jesũ, nĩakenire mũno, tondũ kwa ihinda iraaya nĩakoretwo akĩenda kũmuona. Kũringana na ũrĩa aiguĩte ũhoro wake, aarĩ na mwĩhoko wa kũmuona akĩringa ciama.
English: Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad, for he had wanted to see him for a long time, because he had heard many things about him. He hoped to see some miracle done by him.

Kikuyu: Akĩũria Jesũ ciũria nyingĩ, nowe Jesũ ndaigana kũmũcookeria o na kĩmwe.
English: He questioned him with many words, but he gave no answers.

Kikuyu: Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na arutani a watho maarũgamĩte hau, makĩmũthitanga magwatĩirie mũno.
English: The chief priests and the scribes stood, vehemently accusing him.

Kikuyu: Nake Herode na thigari ciake makĩmũthekerera na makĩmũnyũrũria. Makĩmũhumba nguo yarĩ thaka, na makĩmũcookia kũrĩ Pilato.
English: Herod with his soldiers humiliated him and mocked him. Dressing him in luxurious clothing, they sent him back to Pilate.

Kikuyu: Nao Herode na Pilato magĩtuma ũrata mũthenya ũcio, tondũ mbere ĩyo maatũũrĩte marĩ ũthũ.
English: Herod and Pilate became friends with each other that very day, for before that they were enemies with each other.

Kikuyu: Pilato agĩĩta athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na aathani, na andũ hamwe,
English: Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people,

Kikuyu: akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũnde- heire mũndũ ũyũ atĩ nĩ mũndũ ũhĩtithagia andũ. Na niĩ ndatuĩria ũhoro wake mũrĩ ho, no ndirĩ ũndũ mũũru ndoona wa maũndũ marĩa mũmũthitangĩire.
English: and said to them, “You brought this man to me as one that perverts the people, and behold, having examined him before you, I found no basis for a charge against this man concerning those things of which you accuse him.

Kikuyu: O nake Herode ndanamuona arĩ na mahĩtia, nĩgũkorwo nĩamũcookia kũrĩ ithuĩ; nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ndarĩ ũndũ ekĩte wa gũtũma ooragwo.
English: Neither has Herod, for I sent you to him, and see, nothing worthy of death has been done by him.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngũmũhũũrithia, njooke ndĩmũrekererie.” (
English: I will therefore chastise him and release him.”

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo, kwarĩ mũtugo amohorere mũndũ ũmwe hĩndĩ ĩyo ya Iruga.)
English: Now he had to release one prisoner to them at the feast.†

Kikuyu: No andũ acio makĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo ũmwe, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Twehererie mũndũ ũyũ! Tuohorere Baraba!” (
English: But they all cried out together, saying, “Away with this man! Release to us Barabbas!”—

Kikuyu: Baraba aikĩtio njeera nĩ ũndũ wa kũrutithia ngũĩ itũũra-inĩ rĩu inene, na nĩ ũndũ wa kũũragana.)
English: one who was thrown into prison for a certain revolt in the city, and for murder.

Kikuyu: Nake Pilato, tondũ nĩeendaga kũrekereria Jesũ, akĩmaarĩria o rĩngĩ.
English: Then Pilate spoke to them again, wanting to release Jesus,

Kikuyu: No-o magĩthiĩ na mbere kwanĩrĩra, makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Mwambe mũtĩ-igũrũ! Mwambe mũtĩ-igũrũ!”
English: but they shouted, saying, “Crucify! Crucify him!”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmooria riita rĩa gatatũ atĩrĩ, “Nĩkĩ? Nĩ ũũru ũrĩkũ mũndũ ũyũ eekĩte? Ndionete gĩtũmi gĩa gũtũma ooragwo. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngũmũhũũrithia, njooke ndĩmuohore.”
English: He said to them the third time, “Why? What evil has this man done? I have found no capital crime in him. I will therefore chastise him and release him.”

Kikuyu: No-o makĩanĩrĩra mategũtigithĩria makiugaga aambwo mũtĩ-igũrũ, nayo mĩgambo ĩyo yao ĩkĩhootana.
English: But they were urgent with loud voices, asking that he might be crucified. Their voices and the voices of the chief priests prevailed.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Pilato agĩtua nĩekũmahingĩria o ũguo moorĩtie.
English: Pilate decreed that what they asked for should be done.

Kikuyu: Akĩohora mũndũ ũcio waikĩtio njeera nĩ ũndũ wa kũrutithia ngũĩ na kũũragana, o ũcio moigĩte mohorerwo, nake Pilato akĩneana Jesũ ekwo kũringana na ũrĩa mendaga.
English: He released him who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, for whom they asked, but he delivered Jesus up to their will.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa thigari cioimagaragia Jesũ, ikĩnyiita mũndũ woimaga mĩgũnda-inĩ wetagwo Simoni wa kuuma Kurene. Nacio ikĩmũigĩrĩra mũtharaba wa Jesũ aũkuue na amuume thuutha.
English: When they led him away, they grabbed one Simon of Cyrene, coming from the country, and laid the cross on him to carry it after Jesus.

Kikuyu: Andũ gĩkundi kĩnene makĩmũrũmĩrĩra, hamwe na atumia maagirĩkaga na makarĩra mũno.
English: A great multitude of the people followed him, including women who also mourned and lamented him.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩĩhũgũra na kũrĩ o, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ aarĩ a Jerusalemu, tigai kũndĩrĩra; mwĩrĩrĩrei inyuĩ ene na ciana cianyu.
English: But Jesus, turning to them, said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, don’t weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo kũrĩ hĩndĩ ĩgooka rĩrĩa mũkoigaga atĩrĩ, ‘Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ atumia arĩa thaata, na nda iria itarĩ ciaciara, o na nyondo iria itarĩ ciongwo!’
English: For behold, the days are coming in which they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.’

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo “ ‘nĩmakeera irĩma atĩrĩ, “Tũgwĩrei!” o na macooke meere tũrĩma atĩrĩ, “Tũhumbĩrei!” ’
English: Then they will begin to tell the mountains, ‘Fall on us!’ and tell the hills, ‘Cover us.’*

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, andũ mangĩkorwo nĩmareeka maũndũ maya rĩrĩa mũtĩ ũrĩ mũigũ-rĩ, gũgakĩhaana atĩa woma?”
English: For if they do these things in the green tree, what will be done in the dry?”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, andũ angĩ eerĩ ageri ngero, o nao nĩmatwarirwo hamwe nake makooragwo.
English: There were also others, two criminals, led with him to be put to death.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maakinyire karĩma-inĩ handũ heetagwo Ihĩndĩ-rĩa-Mũtwe, makĩmwamba ho hamwe na ageri ngero acio, ũmwe mwena wake wa ũrĩo, na ũrĩa ũngĩ mwena wake wa ũmotho.
English: When they came to the place that is called “The Skull”, they crucified him there with the criminals, one on the right and the other on the left.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akiuga atĩrĩ, “Baba mohere, nĩgũkorwo matiũĩ ũrĩa mareeka.” Nao magĩcuukĩra nguo ciake mĩtĩ, magĩcigayana.
English: Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing.” Dividing his garments among them, they cast lots.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ nĩmarũgamĩte hau makĩmwĩrorera: Nao anene nĩmamũnyũrũragia makoiga atĩrĩ, “Nĩarahonokagia andũ arĩa angĩ, nĩehonokie aakorwo nĩwe Kristũ wa Ngai, Ũrĩa wake Mũthuure.”
English: The people stood watching. The rulers with them also scoffed at him, saying, “He saved others. Let him save himself, if this is the Christ of God, his chosen one!”

Kikuyu: Thigari o nacio igĩũka, ikĩmũnyũrũria. Ikĩmũkundia thiki,*
English: The soldiers also mocked him, coming to him and offering him vinegar,

Kikuyu: ikĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Akorwo nĩwe Mũthamaki wa Ayahudi, wĩhonokie.”
English: and saying, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”

Kikuyu: Na nĩ haarĩ maandĩko maandĩkĩtwo igũrũ rĩake atĩrĩ: ŨYŨ NĨWE MŨTHAMAKI WA AYAHUDI.
English: An inscription was also written over him in letters of Greek, Latin, and Hebrew: “THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

Kikuyu: Ũmwe wa ageri ngero acio maambanĩirio nake akĩmũruma, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Githĩ wee tiwe Kristũ? Wĩhonokie, na ũtũhonokie!”
English: One of the criminals who was hanged insulted him, saying, “If you are the Christ, save yourself and us!”

Kikuyu: No mũgeri ngero ũcio ũngĩ akĩmũkaania, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ndũngĩĩtigĩra Ngai, kuona atĩ ũtuĩrĩirwo o ũrĩa atuĩrĩirwo?
English: But the other answered, and rebuking him said, “Don’t you even fear God, seeing you are under the same condemnation?

Kikuyu: Ithuĩ tũherithĩtio na kĩhooto, nĩgũkorwo tũraherithĩrio ũrĩa twĩkĩte. No rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũyũ ndarĩ ũndũ mũũru ekĩte.”
English: And we indeed justly, for we receive the due reward for our deeds, but this man has done nothing wrong.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩĩra Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Ĩtĩkĩra gũkandirikana rĩrĩa ũgooka ũthamaki-inĩ waku.”
English: He said to Jesus, “Lord, remember me when you come into your Kingdom.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ngũkwĩra na ma, ũmũthĩ nĩũrĩkorwo hamwe na niĩ ikenero.”
English: Jesus said to him, “Assuredly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, kwarĩ ta thaa thita cia mũthenya, naguo bũrũri wothe ũkĩgĩa nduma nginya thaa kenda,
English: It was now about the sixth hour,‡ and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour.§

Kikuyu: tondũ riũa nĩrĩatigire kwara. Nakĩo gĩtambaya kĩa hekarũ gĩgĩatũkana maita meerĩ.
English: The sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was torn in two.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo mũnene, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Baba, roho wakwa ndaũiga moko-inĩ maku.” Aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, agĩtuĩkana.
English: Jesus, crying with a loud voice, said, “Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!” Having said this, he breathed his last.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũnene wa gĩkundi gĩa thigari igana oonire ũrĩa gwekĩka, akĩgooca Ngai, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ti- itheru ũyũ oima mũndũ mũthingu.”
English: When the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, “Certainly this was a righteous man.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa andũ arĩa othe maarũgamĩte hau kwĩrorera moonire ũrĩa gwekĩka, makĩinũka methikĩire, na makĩĩhũũraga ithũri nĩ kĩeha.
English: All the multitudes that came together to see this, when they saw the things that were done, returned home beating their chests.

Kikuyu: No arĩa othe maamũũĩ, o hamwe na atumia arĩa maamũrũmĩrĩire kuuma Galili, makĩrũgama o haraaya, meroreire maũndũ macio.
English: All his acquaintances and the women who followed with him from Galilee stood at a distance, watching these things.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩ haarĩ na mũndũ wetagwo Jusufu, mũthuuri wa Kĩama, na aarĩ mũndũ mwega na mũrũngĩrĩru,
English: Behold, there was a man named Joseph, who was a member of the council, a good and righteous man

Kikuyu: nake ndetĩkanĩtie na ũguo maatuĩte na mageeka. Oimĩte itũũra rĩa Arimathea, bũrũri wa Judea, na nĩetagĩrĩra ũthamaki wa Ngai.
English: (he had not consented to their counsel and deed), from Arimathaea, a city of the Jews, who was also waiting for God’s Kingdom.

Kikuyu: Nake agĩthiĩ kũrĩ Pilato, akĩhooya etĩkĩrio akuue mwĩrĩ wa Jesũ.†
English: This man went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body.

Kikuyu: Nake agĩcuurũria mwĩrĩ wa Jesũ mũtharaba-inĩ, akĩũkũnja na taama wa gatani, agĩcooka akĩũiga thĩinĩ wa mbĩrĩra yenjetwo rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga, nayo ndĩakoretwo ĩigĩtwo mũndũ.
English: He took it down and wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid him in a tomb that was cut in stone, where no one had ever been laid.

Kikuyu: Na mũthenya ũcio warĩ wa Ihaarĩria, nayo Thabatũ yarĩ hakuhĩ kwambĩrĩria.
English: It was the day of the Preparation, and the Sabbath was drawing near.

Kikuyu: Nao atumia arĩa moimĩte na Jesũ Galili makĩrũmĩrĩra Jusufu, makĩona mbĩrĩra na makĩona ũrĩa mwĩrĩ wake waigirwo ho.
English: The women who had come with him out of Galilee followed after, and saw the tomb and how his body was laid.

Kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩinũka, makĩhaarĩria mahuti manungi wega na maguta manungi wega. No rĩrĩ, mũthenya wa Thabatũ makĩhurũka kũringana na ũrĩa gwathanĩtwo.
English: They returned and prepared spices and ointments. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya wa kwambĩrĩria kiumia, rũciinĩ tene, atumia magĩthiĩ mbĩrĩra-inĩ makuuĩte indo nungi wega iria maathondekete.
English: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they and some others came to the tomb, bringing the spices which they had prepared.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩkora ihiga rĩgaragarĩtio rĩkeherio mũromo-inĩ wa mbĩrĩra,
English: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.

Kikuyu: no rĩrĩa maatoonyire, matiigana gũkora mwĩrĩ wa Mwathani Jesũ.
English: They entered in, and didn’t find the Lord Jesus’ body.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa maagegete nĩ ũhoro ũcio, makiumĩrĩrwo o rĩmwe nĩ arũme eerĩ mehumbĩte nguo njerũ ciakengaga mũno, makĩrũgama hakuhĩ nao.
English: While they were greatly perplexed about this, behold, two men stood by them in dazzling clothing.

Kikuyu: Nao atumia acio metigĩrĩte mũno, makĩinamĩrĩra, magĩturumithia mothiũ mao thĩ, no arũme acio makĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũracaria ũrĩa ũrĩ muoyo kũrĩ arĩa akuũ nĩkĩ?
English: Becoming terrified, they bowed their faces down to the earth. The men said to them, “Why do you seek the living among the dead?

Kikuyu: Ndarĩ haha; nĩariũkĩte! Ririkanai ũrĩa aamwĩrire hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aarĩ na inyuĩ Galili atĩ:
English: He isn’t here, but is risen. Remember what he told you when he was still in Galilee,

Kikuyu: ‘Mũrũ wa Mũndũ no nginya aneanwo moko-inĩ ma andũ ehia, aambwo mũtĩ-igũrũ, na mũthenya wa gatatũ ariũke.’ ”
English: saying that the Son of Man must be delivered up into the hands of sinful men and be crucified, and the third day rise again?”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo makĩririkana ciugo ciake.
English: They remembered his words,

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, maacooka kuuma mbĩrĩra-inĩ, makĩhe arutwo arĩa ikũmi na ũmwe na arĩa angĩ othe ũhoro ũcio.
English: returned from the tomb, and told all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.

Kikuyu: Mariamu Mũmagidali, na Joana, na Mariamu nyina wa Jakubu, na atumia arĩa angĩ maarĩ nao, nĩo meerire atũmwo ũhoro ũcio.
English: Now they were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, and Mary the mother of James. The other women with them told these things to the apostles.

Kikuyu: No-o matiigana gwĩtĩkia atumia acio, tondũ ũhoro ũcio wahaanaga ta ũrimũ kũrĩ o.
English: These words seemed to them to be nonsense, and they didn’t believe them.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Petero agĩũkĩra, agĩtengʼera mbĩrĩra-inĩ. Na ainamĩrĩra akĩona mataama ma gatani maigĩtwo thĩ marĩ moiki, agĩĩthiĩra agegearĩte nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo gwekĩkĩte.
English: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb. Stooping and looking in, he saw the strips of linen lying by themselves, and he departed to his home, wondering what had happened.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya o ro ũcio arutwo eerĩ a Jesũ maarĩ rũgendo-inĩ magĩthiĩ itũũra rĩetagwo Emau, rĩarĩ ta kilomita ikũmi kuuma Jerusalemu.
English: Behold, two of them were going that very day to a village named Emmaus, which was sixty stadia† from Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Nao nĩmaragia ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa mothe meekĩkĩte.
English: They talked with each other about all of these things which had happened.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa maathiiaga makĩaragia ũhoro ũcio, Jesũ akĩmoimĩrĩra, agĩtwarana nao;
English: While they talked and questioned together, Jesus himself came near, and went with them.

Kikuyu: no matiigana kũrekwo mamũmenye.
English: But their eyes were kept from recognizing him.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũhoro ũrĩkũ ũcio mũrathiĩ mũkĩaragia?” Nao makĩrũgama matukĩte mothiũ.
English: He said to them, “What are you talking about as you walk, and are sad?”

Kikuyu: Ũmwe wao, wetagwo Kileopa akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wee kaĩ ũrĩ mũgeni Jerusalemu ũtamenyete maũndũ marĩa mekĩkĩte kuo matukũ-inĩ maya?”
English: One of them, named Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only stranger in Jerusalem who doesn’t know the things which have happened there in these days?”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ maũndũ marĩkũ?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũhoro wa Jesũ wa Nazarethi, ũrĩa ũraarĩ mũnabii ũrĩ na ũhoti wa ciugo iria araaragia na ciĩko iria areekaga arĩ mbere ya Ngai o na mbere ya andũ othe.
English: He said to them, “What things?” They said to him, “The things concerning Jesus the Nazarene, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people;

Kikuyu: Athĩnjĩri- Ngai arĩa anene na aathani aitũ maramũneanire atuĩrwo kũũragwo, na maramwambithia mũtĩ-igũrũ.
English: and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him up to be condemned to death, and crucified him.

Kikuyu: No ithuĩ-rĩ, tũrehokaga atĩ ũcio nĩwe ũgũkũũra andũ Isiraeli. Makĩria ma ũguo, rĩu nĩ mũthenya wa ĩtatũ kuuma maũndũ maya mothe mekĩka.
English: But we were hoping that it was he who would redeem Israel. Yes, and besides all this, it is now the third day since these things happened.

Kikuyu: Hamwe na ũguo atumia amwe aitũ nĩmatũmakirie mũno tondũ megũthiĩte mbĩrĩra-inĩ rũciinĩ tene,
English: Also, certain women of our company amazed us, having arrived early at the tomb;

Kikuyu: no matinona mwĩrĩ wake. Mokire matwĩra atĩ nĩmonire kĩoneki kĩa araika, arĩa mameerire atĩ arĩ muoyo.
English: and when they didn’t find his body, they came saying that they had also seen a vision of angels, who said that he was alive.

Kikuyu: Nao amwe a thiritũ iitũ nĩmathiire mbĩrĩra-inĩ, na makora o ũguo atumia acio mekuugĩte, no rĩrĩ, we mwene matinamuona.”
English: Some of us went to the tomb and found it just like the women had said, but they didn’t see him.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ andũ aya mwagĩte ũũgĩ na mũtihiũhaga gwĩtĩkia maũndũ mothe marĩa anabii moigĩte!
English: He said to them, “Foolish people, and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken!

Kikuyu: Githĩ to mũhaka Kristũ ambe anyariirwo na acooke atoonye riiri-inĩ wake?”
English: Didn’t the Christ have to suffer these things and to enter into his glory?”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmataarĩria ũhoro wothe ũrĩa waarĩtio Maandĩko-inĩ ũmũkoniĩ, ambĩrĩirie na maandĩko ma Musa na ma Anabii othe.
English: Beginning from Moses and from all the prophets, he explained to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.

Kikuyu: Na maakuhĩrĩria itũũra rĩrĩa maathiiaga, Jesũ agĩtua taarĩ kũhĩtũka arahĩtũka.
English: They came near to the village where they were going, and he acted like he would go further.

Kikuyu: No makĩmũringĩrĩria na hinya, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Raarĩrĩra gũkũ na ithuĩ, nĩkũratuka na gũkiriĩ kũgĩa nduma.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩtoonya aikaranie nao.
English: They urged him, saying, “Stay with us, for it is almost evening, and the day is almost over.” He went in to stay with them.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aikarire metha-inĩ hamwe nao, akĩoya mũgate, agĩcookia ngaatho, akĩwenyũranga, akĩmahe.
English: When he had sat down at the table with them, he took the bread and gave thanks. Breaking it, he gave it to them.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo makĩhingũka maitho, makĩmũmenya, nake akĩbuĩria, makĩaga kũmuona rĩngĩ.
English: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him; then he vanished out of their sight.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Githĩ ngoro ciitũ itiikũhĩahĩaga hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ekwaragia na ithuĩ njĩra-inĩ, na agĩtũtaarĩria ũrĩa Maandĩko moigĩte?”
English: They said to one another, “Weren’t our hearts burning within us while he spoke to us along the way, and while he opened the Scriptures to us?”

Kikuyu: Nao magĩũkĩra o hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩcooka Jerusalemu. Kũu magĩkora arutwo arĩa ikũmi na ũmwe, hamwe na arĩa maarĩ nao monganĩte,
English: They rose up that very hour, returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and those who were with them,

Kikuyu: makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ma! Mwathani nĩariũkĩte na nĩoimĩrĩire Simoni.”
English: saying, “The Lord is risen indeed, and has appeared to Simon!”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo acio eerĩ makĩmeera maũndũ marĩa meekĩkĩte marĩ njĩra-inĩ, na ũrĩa maamenyire Jesũ hĩndĩ ĩrĩa eenyũrire mũgate.
English: They related the things that happened along the way, and how he was recognized by them in the breaking of the bread.

Kikuyu: O makĩaragia ũhoro ũcio, Jesũ akĩrũgama gatagatĩ kao akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thayũ ũrogĩa na inyuĩ.”
English: As they said these things, Jesus himself stood among them, and said to them, “Peace be to you.”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩhahũka na makĩmaka, magĩĩciiria atĩ nĩ ngoma moonaga.
English: But they were terrified and filled with fear, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũratangĩka nĩkĩ, na nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma mũgĩe na nganja meciiria-inĩ manyu?
English: He said to them, “Why are you troubled? Why do doubts arise in your hearts?

Kikuyu: Ta rorai moko makwa na nyarĩrĩ ciakwa. Nĩ niĩ mwene! Huutiai muone; roho ndũrĩ nyama na mahĩndĩ, ta ũrĩa mũrona ndĩ nacio.”
English: See my hands and my feet, that it is truly me. Touch me and see, for a spirit doesn’t have flesh and bones, as you see that I have.”

Kikuyu: Aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, akĩmoonia moko make na nyarĩrĩ ciake.
English: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩkorwo matarĩ maretĩkia nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa makenete na makagega. Nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũrĩ na kĩndũ gĩa kũrĩa?”
English: While they still didn’t believe for joy, and wondered, he said to them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũnengera gĩcunjĩ gĩa thamaki hĩu.
English: They gave him a piece of a broiled fish and some honeycomb.

Kikuyu: Nake agĩkĩoya, akĩrĩa makĩĩonagĩra.
English: He took them, and ate in front of them.

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũũ nĩguo ndamwĩrire hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndaarĩ na inyuĩ atĩ: Maũndũ mothe marĩa mandĩkĩtwo ũhoro wakwa Watho-inĩ wa Musa, na Maandĩko-inĩ ma Anabii, na Thaburi, no nginya mahingio.”
English: He said to them, “This is what I told you while I was still with you, that all things which are written in the law of Moses, the prophets, and the psalms concerning me must be fulfilled.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo akĩhingũra meciiria mao nĩguo mamenye Maandĩko.
English: Then he opened their minds, that they might understand the Scriptures.

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũũ nĩguo kwandĩkĩtwo: Kristũ nĩakanyariirwo na ariũkio kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ mũthenya wa gatatũ,
English: He said to them, “Thus it is written, and thus it was necessary for the Christ to suffer and to rise from the dead the third day,

Kikuyu: naguo ũhoro wa kwĩrira na kũrekerwo kwa mehia ũkaahunjĩrio ndũrĩrĩ ciothe thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩake, wambĩrĩirie Jerusalemu.
English: and that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name to all the nations, beginning at Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ mũrĩ aira a maũndũ macio.
English: You are witnesses of these things.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩngũmũtũmĩra kĩrĩa Baba eranĩire; no ikarai thĩinĩ wa itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene nginya rĩrĩa mũkaaheo hinya kuuma Igũrũ.”
English: Behold, I send out the promise of my Father on you. But wait in the city of Jerusalem until you are clothed with power from on high.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmatongoria kuuma kũu magĩthiĩ magĩkinya hakuhĩ na Bethania; akĩoya moko na igũrũ, akĩmarathima.
English: He led them out as far as Bethany, and he lifted up his hands and blessed them.

Kikuyu: Na o akĩmarathimaga, akĩmatiga, akĩiyũkio akĩambata igũrũ.
English: While he blessed them, he withdrew from them and was carried up into heaven.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũgooca na magĩcooka Jerusalemu makenete mũno.
English: They worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,

Kikuyu: Nao nĩmaikaraga hekarũ-inĩ mahinda mothe makĩgoocaga Ngai.
English: and were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.
